what: How to add support for an R function in tidypolars?
title: Contributing to tidypolars
text: use function supported, tidypolars throw error. doesnвҖҷt mean tidypolars support , simply implemented yet. tidypolars can technically support hundreds functions manipulate numbers, strings, dates, limited tidyverse. first step come across unsupported function find corresponding syntax polars. Just example, suppose stringr::str_ends() yet supported (actually ). first define simple test data.frame DataFrame counterpart polars: , find polars syntax gives output stringr::str_ends(). case, polars string methods $ends_with(): interests us expression inside $with_columns() call. Now know polars translation, letвҖҷs put function tidypolars know. Internally, tidypolars prefix function name (str_ends) pl_ look translated functions. Depending function translate, can stored different places. function works strings, can store R/funs-string.R. add support negate argument, look like: can notice new function must named pl_<function_name>_<package_name>. multiple packages can export functions name (example data.table::() dplyr::()). Therefore, want ensure translation called function comes specific package. check_empty_dots(...) grab additional arguments stringr::str_ends() might ignore counterparts polars (also adds message warning user arguments used). package reloaded, can use function tidypolars workflow: Finally, thing left add tests tests/testthat folder.
code: library(dplyr, warn.conflicts = FALSE) library(stringr) library(polars)  test <- tibble(x = c("abc", "a1", "dac")) test_pl <- pl$DataFrame(x = c("abc", "a1", "dac")) test |>   mutate(ends_with_c = str_ends(x, "c")) ## # A tibble: 3 Г— 2 ##   x     ends_with_c ##   <chr> <lgl>       ## 1 abc   TRUE        ## 2 a1    FALSE       ## 3 dac   TRUE test_pl$with_columns(   ends_with_c = pl$col("x")$str$ends_with("c") ) ## shape: (3, 2) ## в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ ## в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ ends_with_c в”Ӯ ## в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---         в”Ӯ ## в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ bool        в”Ӯ ## в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў ## в”Ӯ abc в”Ҷ true        в”Ӯ ## в”Ӯ a1  в”Ҷ false       в”Ӯ ## в”Ӯ dac в”Ҷ true        в”Ӯ ## в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ pl_str_ends_stringr <- function(string, pattern, negate = FALSE, ...) {   check_empty_dots(...)   out <- string$str$ends_with(pattern)   if (isTRUE(negate)) {     out$not()   } else {     out   } } library(tidypolars, warn.conflicts = FALSE)  test_pl |>   mutate(ends_with_c = str_ends(x, "c")) ## shape: (3, 2) ## в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ ## в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ ends_with_c в”Ӯ ## в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---         в”Ӯ ## в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ bool        в”Ӯ ## в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў ## в”Ӯ abc в”Ҷ true        в”Ӯ ## в”Ӯ a1  в”Ҷ false       в”Ӯ ## в”Ӯ dac в”Ҷ true        в”Ӯ ## в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

title: Contributing to tidypolars
text: tidypolars uses testthat check functions expected output. changes affect behavior functions (example adding extra argument), add tests related file tests/testthat. tidyverse functions apply entire dataframe (e.g., drop_na()) test file (test-drop_na.R case). Functions apply one multiple vectors (.e.В functions used mutate(), summarize(), etc.) tested specific files. added function R/funs-string.R, add tests tests/testthat/test-funs_string.R (datetime functions, etc.). test rules: test data must always ) tibble named test ii) Polars DataFrame named test_pl. Always compare tidypolars tidyverse code directly, compare tidypolars output hard-coded values. Use expect_equal() tidyverse tidypolars code give output. Use expect_both_error() tidyverse tidypolars code error (ensure error actually due tested function typo instance). Use expect_equal_or_both_error() property-based testing since control input case. matter type function added modified, write tests Polars DataFrames. tests automatically copied modified devtools::test() run LazyFrames well (instance, file test-drop_na-lazy.R automatically generated).

title: Contributing to tidypolars
text: Please follow Conventional Commits PR titles, meaning PR titles must start вҖңfeat:вҖқ, вҖңfix:вҖқ, another appropriate name (see linked documentation). example: feat: new feature. fix: bug fix. docs: Documentation changes. test: Adding missing tests correcting existing tests. chore: Changes build process auxiliary tools libraries. refactor: code change neither fixes bug adds feature. See list previous PRs examples.

what: Do not include as_polars_df() or as_polars_lf() in the timing
title: How to benchmark tidypolars
text: benchmarks something like following: issue approach as_polars_df() converts R data.frame Polars DataFrame, takes time. highlighted вҖңGetting startedвҖқ vignette, as_polars_df() as_polars_lf() convenience functions demo testing purposes. recommended way use tidypolars using dedicated readers (scan_parquet_polars(), read_parquet_polars(), etc.) import data directly Polars DataFrame LazyFrame. Using as_polars_df() as_polars_lf() fine get data ready benchmarked, operations included timing.
code: my_function <- function(dat) {   dat <- as_polars_df(dat)   dat |>     <some slow operation> }  bench::mark(   my_function(my_r_data_frame) )

title: How to benchmark tidypolars
text: Polars provides DataFrames LazyFrames. Operations DataFrames executed вҖңeager modeвҖқ, meaning optimization happening behind scenes, instance efficiently reorder operations. real-life workflows, strongly recommended use LazyFrames since allow large number optimizations. benchmarks demos, therefore prefer as_polars_lf() as_polars_df(). Using LazyFrames means also need collect results trigger computation. can done using compute() (return Polars DataFrame), collect() (return R data.frame), as_tibble() (return tibble). Depending objective benchmark, three options can valid. compute() faster avoids extra step converting Polars DataFrame R. However, output compute() passed directly ggplot2, example. summary, instead : :
code: my_polars_data <- as_polars_df(my_r_data_frame)  my_function <- function(dat) {   my_polars_data |>     <some slow operation> }  bench::mark(   my_function(my_polars_data) ) my_polars_data <- as_polars_lf(my_r_data_frame) # Use as_polars_lf()  my_function <- function(dat) {   my_polars_data |>     <some slow operation> |>     compute() # Or collect() or as_tibble() }  bench::mark(   my_function(my_polars_data) )

title: How to benchmark tidypolars
text: addition lazy execution, Polars comes streaming engine able perform operations larger--RAM data. also faster default engine many cases, just larger--RAM data. Note: time writing (August 2025), streaming engine yet used default, become default next months. Using extremely simple: pass engine = "streaming" compute(), collect() as_tibble().

what: Do not try to run tidypolars in parallel
title: How to benchmark tidypolars
text: benchmarks take advantage tools future mirai run code parallel. However, tidypolars shouldnвҖҷt used tools. polars code used internally already optimized run cores isnвҖҷt guaranteed play well parallel frameworks. cases, may want use frameworks Polars objects, exception rather rule.

what: How does tidypolars translate R expressions into Polars expressions?
title: R and Polars expressions
text: tidypolars receives expression, runs function translate() several times components translated Polars equivalent. four possible components: single values, column names, external objects, functions.

previous_headings: How does tidypolars translate R expressions into Polars expressions?
what: Single values, column names, and external objects
title: R and Polars expressions
text: pass single value, like x = 1 x = "", wrapped pl$lit(). also case external objects difference need wrapped {{ }} evaluated wrapped pl$lit(). Column names, like x = mpg, wrapped pl$col().
code: x = "a"               ->  x = pl$lit("a") x = {{ some_value }}  ->  x = pl$lit(*value*) x = mpg               ->  x = pl$col("mpg")

code: library(collapse, warn.conflicts = FALSE) #> collapse 2.1.5, see ?`collapse-package` or ?`collapse-documentation` library(dplyr, warn.conflicts = FALSE) library(dtplyr) library(polars) library(tidypolars)  large_iris <- data.table::rbindlist(rep(list(iris), 100000)) large_iris_pl <- as_polars_lf(large_iris) large_iris_dt <- lazy_dt(large_iris)  format(nrow(large_iris), big.mark = ",") #> [1] "15,000,000"  bench::mark(   polars = {     large_iris_pl$       select(c("Sepal.Length", "Sepal.Width", "Petal.Length", "Petal.Width"))$       with_columns(         pl$when(           (pl$col("Petal.Length") / pl$col("Petal.Width") > 3)         )$then(pl$lit("long"))$           otherwise(pl$lit("large"))$           alias("petal_type")       )$       filter(pl$col("Sepal.Length")$is_between(4.5, 5.5))$       collect()   },   tidypolars = {     large_iris_pl |>       select(starts_with(c("Sep", "Pet"))) |>       mutate(         petal_type = ifelse((Petal.Length / Petal.Width) > 3, "long", "large")       ) |>       filter(between(Sepal.Length, 4.5, 5.5)) |>       compute()   },   dplyr = {     large_iris |>       select(starts_with(c("Sep", "Pet"))) |>       mutate(         petal_type = ifelse((Petal.Length / Petal.Width) > 3, "long", "large")       ) |>       filter(between(Sepal.Length, 4.5, 5.5))   },   dtplyr = {     large_iris_dt |>       select(starts_with(c("Sep", "Pet"))) |>       mutate(         petal_type = ifelse((Petal.Length / Petal.Width) > 3, "long", "large")       ) |>       filter(between(Sepal.Length, 4.5, 5.5)) |>       as.data.frame()   },   collapse = {     large_iris |>       fselect(c("Sepal.Length", "Sepal.Width", "Petal.Length", "Petal.Width")) |>       fmutate(         petal_type = data.table::fifelse((Petal.Length / Petal.Width) > 3, "long", "large")       ) |>       fsubset(Sepal.Length >= 4.5 & Sepal.Length <= 5.5)   },   check = FALSE,   iterations = 40 ) #> Warning: Some expressions had a GC in every iteration; so filtering is disabled. #> # A tibble: 5 Г— 6 #>   expression      min   median `itr/sec` mem_alloc `gc/sec` #>   <bch:expr> <bch:tm> <bch:tm>     <dbl> <bch:byt>    <dbl> #> 1 polars     140.93ms 151.97ms     6.34     2.32MB    0.158 #> 2 tidypolars 145.25ms 163.29ms     5.45     1.24MB    0.545 #> 3 dplyr         1.72s    1.88s     0.519    1.79GB    1.41 #> 4 dtplyr     751.04ms 920.18ms     1.10     1.72GB    2.97 #> 5 collapse   387.92ms 457.01ms     2.20   745.96MB    1.10  # NOTE: do NOT take the "mem_alloc" results into account. # `bench::mark()` doesn't report the accurate memory usage for packages calling # Rust code.

title: More Efficient Tidyverse Code, Using Polars in the Background
text: tidypolars built polars, available CRAN. means tidypolars also canвҖҷt CRAN. However, can install R-universe. development version contains latest improvements bug fixes:
code: Sys.setenv(NOT_CRAN = "true") install.packages("tidypolars", repos = c("https://community.r-multiverse.org", 'https://cloud.r-project.org')) # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github(   "etiennebacher/tidypolars",   repos = c("https://community.r-multiverse.org", 'https://cloud.r-project.org') )

title: More Efficient Tidyverse Code, Using Polars in the Background
text: Several packages developed handle large data efficiently keeping tidyverse syntax: tidypolars different?: Polars (therefore tidypolars) uses unofficial Arrow memory specification. operations implemented (optimized) scratch, meaning query optimizations can different Acero. list R functions translated Arrow engine may also differ. tidypolars different?: tidypolars provides lazy evaluation memory-efficient since doesnвҖҷt import data memory. also provides streaming engine handle larger--RAM datasets. tidypolars different?: tidypolars doesnвҖҷt translate R code SQL directly evaluates Polars. tidypolars different?: collapse. tidypolars different?: similar arrow, list R functions optimized Polars DuckDB isnвҖҷt identical use case determine tool runs fastest. duckplyr also relies fallback mechanism run code вҖңstandard RвҖқ function translated. tidypolars conservative error case, avoiding importing data may crash session size. tidypolars different?: tidypolars doesnвҖҷt need installing another tool focuses processing data single machine, distributed processing. Therefore, need handle data larger memory, three options: arrow, duckplyr, tidypolars. best one probably depend use case constraints (e.g.В tidypolars available via R-universe isnвҖҷt CRAN). Regarding performance, one refer DuckDB benchmarks compare tools. Keep mind accurately benchmarking data processing tools hard; benchmarks give useful information donвҖҷt necessarily apply contexts.

code: pl_test <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   x1 = c("a", "a", "b", "a", "c"),   x2 = c(2, 1, 5, 3, 1),   value = sample(1:5) )  arrange(pl_test, x1) #> shape: (5, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x1  в”Ҷ x2  в”Ҷ value в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ i32   в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 4     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 3     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ 2     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ b   в”Ҷ 5.0 в”Ҷ 1     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ c   в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 5     в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ arrange(pl_test, x1, -x2) #> shape: (5, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x1  в”Ҷ x2  в”Ҷ value в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ i32   в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ 2     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 4     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 3     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ b   в”Ҷ 5.0 в”Ҷ 1     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ c   в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 5     в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # if the data is grouped, you need to specify `.by_group = TRUE` to sort by # the groups first pl_test |>   group_by(x1) |>   arrange(-x2, .by_group = TRUE) #> shape: (5, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x1  в”Ҷ x2  в”Ҷ value в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ i32   в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ 2     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 4     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 3     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ b   в”Ҷ 5.0 в”Ҷ 1     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ c   в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 5     в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ #> Groups [3]: x1 #> Maintain order: FALSE

code: p1 <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   x = sample(letters, 20),   y = sample(1:100, 20) ) p2 <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   z = sample(letters, 20),   w = sample(1:100, 20) )  bind_cols_polars(p1, p2) #> shape: (20, 4) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ z   в”Ҷ w   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ i32 в”Ҷ str в”Ҷ i32 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ d   в”Ҷ 86  в”Ҷ a   в”Ҷ 6   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f   в”Ҷ 43  в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ 22  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i   в”Ҷ 7   в”Ҷ o   в”Ҷ 19  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ e   в”Ҷ 32  в”Ҷ r   в”Ҷ 2   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ w   в”Ҷ 53  в”Ҷ e   в”Ҷ 64  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ вҖҰ   в”Ҷ вҖҰ   в”Ҷ вҖҰ   в”Ҷ вҖҰ   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ y   в”Ҷ 1   в”Ҷ x   в”Ҷ 60  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ z   в”Ҷ 95  в”Ҷ f   в”Ҷ 14  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ n   в”Ҷ 90  в”Ҷ d   в”Ҷ 5   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ c   в”Ҷ 51  в”Ҷ n   в”Ҷ 76  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 46  в”Ҷ t   в”Ҷ 17  в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ bind_cols_polars(list(p1, p2)) #> shape: (20, 4) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ z   в”Ҷ w   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ i32 в”Ҷ str в”Ҷ i32 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ d   в”Ҷ 86  в”Ҷ a   в”Ҷ 6   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f   в”Ҷ 43  в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ 22  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i   в”Ҷ 7   в”Ҷ o   в”Ҷ 19  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ e   в”Ҷ 32  в”Ҷ r   в”Ҷ 2   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ w   в”Ҷ 53  в”Ҷ e   в”Ҷ 64  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ вҖҰ   в”Ҷ вҖҰ   в”Ҷ вҖҰ   в”Ҷ вҖҰ   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ y   в”Ҷ 1   в”Ҷ x   в”Ҷ 60  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ z   в”Ҷ 95  в”Ҷ f   в”Ҷ 14  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ n   в”Ҷ 90  в”Ҷ d   в”Ҷ 5   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ c   в”Ҷ 51  в”Ҷ n   в”Ҷ 76  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 46  в”Ҷ t   в”Ҷ 17  в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

title: Stack multiple Data/LazyFrames on top of each other вҖ” bind_rows_polars
text: ... Polars DataFrames LazyFrames combine. argument can either Data/LazyFrame, list Data/LazyFrames. Columns matched name, missing columns filled NA. .id name optional identifier column. Provide string create output column identifies input. elements ... named, identifier use names. Otherwise, simple count.

title: Stack multiple Data/LazyFrames on top of each other вҖ” bind_rows_polars
code: library(polars) p1 <- pl$DataFrame(   x = c("a", "b"),   y = 1:2 ) p2 <- pl$DataFrame(   y = 3:4,   z = c("c", "d") )$with_columns(pl$col("y")$cast(pl$Int16))  bind_rows_polars(p1, p2) #> shape: (4, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x    в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ z    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ ---  в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str  в”Ҷ i32 в”Ҷ str  в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ a    в”Ҷ 1   в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ b    в”Ҷ 2   в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ null в”Ҷ 3   в”Ҷ c    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ null в”Ҷ 4   в”Ҷ d    в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # this is equivalent bind_rows_polars(list(p1, p2)) #> shape: (4, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x    в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ z    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ ---  в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str  в”Ҷ i32 в”Ҷ str  в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ a    в”Ҷ 1   в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ b    в”Ҷ 2   в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ null в”Ҷ 3   в”Ҷ c    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ null в”Ҷ 4   в”Ҷ d    в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # create an id colum bind_rows_polars(p1, p2, .id = "id") #> shape: (4, 4) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ x    в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ z    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ str  в”Ҷ i32 в”Ҷ str  в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ a    в”Ҷ 1   в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ b    в”Ҷ 2   в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ null в”Ҷ 3   в”Ҷ c    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ null в”Ҷ 4   в”Ҷ d    в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # create an id colum with named elements bind_rows_polars(p1 = p1, p2 = p2, .id = "id") #> shape: (4, 4) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ x    в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ z    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ str  в”Ҷ i32 в”Ҷ str  в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ p1  в”Ҷ a    в”Ҷ 1   в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ p1  в”Ҷ b    в”Ҷ 2   в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ p2  в”Ҷ null в”Ҷ 3   в”Ҷ c    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ p2  в”Ҷ null в”Ҷ 4   в”Ҷ d    в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

title: Complete a data frame with missing combinations of data вҖ” complete.polars_data_frame
text: data Polars Data/LazyFrame ... expression accepted dplyr::select(): variable names, column numbers, select helpers, etc. used continuous variables, may need fill values appear data: use expressions like year = 2010:2020 year = full_seq(year, 1). fill named list variable supplies single value use instead NA missing combinations. explicit implicit (newly created) explicit (pre-existing) missing values filled fill? default, TRUE, set FALSE limit fill implicit missing values.

title: Complete a data frame with missing combinations of data вҖ” complete.polars_data_frame
code: df <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   group = c(1:2, 1, 2),   item_id = c(1:2, 2, 3),   item_name = c("a", "a", "b", "b"),   value1 = c(1, NA, 3, 4),   value2 = 4:7 ) df #> shape: (4, 5) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ group в”Ҷ item_id в”Ҷ item_name в”Ҷ value1 99.0   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2.0   в”Ҷ 3.0     в”Ҷ b         в”Ҷ 4.0    в”Ҷ 7.0    в”Ӯ #> previously implicit) # missing values with `explicit = FALSE` df |>   complete(     group, item_id, item_name,     fill = list(value1 = 0, value2 = 99),     explicit = FALSE   ) #> shape: (12, 5) #> в”Ҷ value2 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ ---   в”Ҷ ---     в”Ҷ ---       в”Ҷ ---    в”Ҷ ---    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64   в”Ҷ f64     в”Ҷ str       в”Ҷ f64    в”Ҷ f64    в”Ӯ #> what: Run computations on a LazyFrame вҖ” compute.polars_lazy_frame
title: Run computations on a LazyFrame вҖ” compute.polars_lazy_frame
text: collect() compute() can applied LazyFrame . check validity query (instance raising error string operation applied numeric column), optimize background, perform computations. two functions differ output type: compute() returns Polars DataFrame; collect() returns tibble::tibble. operation consumes memory takes longer compute() also needs convert data Polars R.

title: Run computations on a LazyFrame вҖ” compute.polars_lazy_frame
code: # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' compute(   x,   ...,   type_coercion = TRUE,   predicate_pushdown = TRUE,   projection_pushdown = TRUE,   simplify_expression = TRUE,   slice_pushdown = TRUE,   comm_subplan_elim = TRUE,   comm_subexpr_elim = TRUE,   cluster_with_columns = TRUE,   no_optimization = FALSE,   engine = c("auto", "in-memory", "streaming"),   streaming = FALSE )  # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' collect(   x,   ...,   type_coercion = TRUE,   predicate_pushdown = TRUE,   projection_pushdown = TRUE,   simplify_expression = TRUE,   slice_pushdown = TRUE,   comm_subplan_elim = TRUE,   comm_subexpr_elim = TRUE,   cluster_with_columns = TRUE,   no_optimization = FALSE,   engine = c("auto", "in-memory", "streaming"),   streaming = FALSE,   .name_repair = "check_unique",   uint8 = "integer",   int64 = "double",   date = "Date",   time = "hms",   decimal = "double",   as_clock_class = FALSE,   ambiguous = "raise",   non_existent = "raise" )

title: Run computations on a LazyFrame вҖ” compute.polars_lazy_frame
text: x Polars LazyFrame ... Dots empty. type_coercion Coerce types operations succeed run minimal required memory (default TRUE). predicate_pushdown Applies filters early possible scan level (default TRUE). projection_pushdown Select columns needed scan level (default TRUE). simplify_expression Various optimizations, constant folding replacing expensive operations faster alternatives (default TRUE). slice_pushdown load required slice scan. materialize sliced outputs level. materialize sliced outputs (default TRUE). comm_subplan_elim Cache branching subplans occur self-joins unions (default TRUE). comm_subexpr_elim Cache common subexpressions (default TRUE). cluster_with_columns Combine sequential independent calls $with_columns(). no_optimization Sets following optimizations FALSE: predicate_pushdown, projection_pushdown,  slice_pushdown, simplify_expression. Default FALSE. engine engine name use processing query. One followings: "auto" (default): Select engine automatically. "-memory" engine selected cases. "-memory": Use -memory engine. "streaming": Use streaming engine, usually faster can handle larger--memory data. streaming Deprecated, use engine instead. .name_repair, uint8, int64, date, time, decimal, as_clock_class, ambiguous, non_existent Parameters control conversion polars types R. See ?polars:::.data.frame.polars_lazy_frame explanations accepted values.

title: Run computations on a LazyFrame вҖ” compute.polars_lazy_frame
text: compute() returns Polars DataFrame, collect() returns tibble.

code: test <- polars::as_polars_df(mtcars)  # grouping variables must be specified in count() and add_count() count(test, cyl) #> shape: (3, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ cyl в”Ҷ n   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ u32 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 4.0 в”Ҷ 11  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 6.0 в”Ҷ 7   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 8.0 в”Ҷ 14  в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ count(test, cyl, am) #> shape: (6, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ cyl в”Ҷ am  в”Ҷ n   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ u32 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 4.0 в”Ҷ 0.0 в”Ҷ 3   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 4.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 8   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 6.0 в”Ҷ 0.0 в”Ҷ 4   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 6.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 3   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 8.0 в”Ҷ 0.0 в”Ҷ 12  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 8.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 2   в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ count(test, cyl, am, sort = TRUE, name = "count") #> shape: в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 8.0 в”Ҷ 0.0 в”Ҷ 12    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 4.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 8     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 6.0 в”Ҷ 0.0 в”Ҷ 4     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 4.0 в”Ҷ 0.0 в”Ҷ 3     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 6.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 3     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 8.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 2     в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  add_count(test, cyl, am, sort = TRUE, name = "count") #> shape: (32, title: Remove or keep only duplicated rows in a Data/LazyFrame вҖ” distinct.polars_data_frame
text: default, duplicates looked variables. possible specify subset variables duplicates looked . also possible keep either first occurrence, last occurence remove duplicates.

title: Remove or keep only duplicated rows in a Data/LazyFrame вҖ” distinct.polars_data_frame
code: # S3 method for class 'polars_data_frame' distinct(.data, ..., .keep_all = FALSE, keep = "first", maintain_order = TRUE)  # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' distinct(.data, ..., .keep_all = FALSE, keep = "first", maintain_order = TRUE)  duplicated_rows(.data, ...)

title: Remove or keep only duplicated rows in a Data/LazyFrame вҖ” distinct.polars_data_frame
text: .data Polars Data/LazyFrame ... expression accepted dplyr::select(): variable names, column numbers, select helpers, etc. .keep_all TRUE, keep variables .data duplicated rows removed. keep Either "first" (keep first occurrence duplicated row), "last" (last occurrence) "none" (remove ofccurences duplicated rows). maintain_order Maintain row order. default can slow process large datasets prevents use streaming.

title: Remove or keep only duplicated rows in a Data/LazyFrame вҖ” distinct.polars_data_frame
code: pl_test <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   iso_o = c(rep(c("AA", "AB"), each = 2), "AC", "DC"),   iso_d = rep(c("BA", "BB", "BC"), each = 2),   value = c(2, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) )  distinct(pl_test) #> shape: (5, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ iso_o в”Ҷ iso_d в”Ҷ value в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ ---   в”Ҷ ---   в”Ҷ ---   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str   в”Ҷ str   в”Ҷ f64   в”Ӯ #> в”Ҷ BC    в”Ҷ 5.0   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ DC    в”Ҷ BC    в”Ҷ 6.0   в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ distinct(pl_test, iso_o) #> shape: (4, 1) #> в”Ҷ ---   в”Ҷ ---   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str   в”Ҷ str   в”Ҷ f64   в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ AA    в”Ҷ BA    в”Ҷ 2.0   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ AA    в”Ҷ BA what: Show the optimized and non-optimized query plans вҖ” explain.polars_lazy_frame
title: Show the optimized and non-optimized query plans вҖ” explain.polars_lazy_frame
text: function available LazyFrames . default, explain() shows query plan optimized run Polars. Setting optimized = FALSE shows query plan -, without optimization done, query performed. Note plans read bottom top.

title: Show the optimized and non-optimized query plans вҖ” explain.polars_lazy_frame
code: # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' explain(x, optimized = TRUE, ...)

title: Show the optimized and non-optimized query plans вҖ” explain.polars_lazy_frame
text: x Polars LazyFrame. optimized Logical. TRUE (default), show query optimized Polars. Otherwise, show initial query. ... Ignored.

title: Show the optimized and non-optimized query plans вҖ” explain.polars_lazy_frame
code:
query <- mtcars |>   as_polars_lf() |>   arrange(drat) |>   filter(cyl == 3) |>   select(mpg)  # unoptimized query plan: no_opt <- explain(query, optimized = FALSE) no_opt #> [1] "SELECT [col("mpg")]
  FILTER [(col("cyl")) == (3.0)]
  FROM
    SELECT [col("mpg"), col("cyl"), col("disp"), col("hp"), col("drat"), col("wt"), col("qsec"), col("vs"), col("am"), col("gear"), col("carb")]
      SORT BY [nulls_last: [true]] [col("__TIDYPOLARS_TEMP_SORT__1")]
         WITH_COLUMNS:
         [col("drat").alias("__TIDYPOLARS_TEMP_SORT__1")] 
          DF ["mpg", "cyl", "disp", "hp", ...]; PROJECT */11 COLUMNS"  # better printing with cat(): cat(no_opt) #> SELECT [col("mpg")] #>   FILTER [(col("cyl")) == (3.0)] #>   FROM #>     SELECT [col("mpg"), col("cyl"), col("disp"), col("hp"), col("drat"), col("wt"), col("qsec"), col("vs"), col("am"), col("gear"), col("carb")] #>       SORT BY [nulls_last: [true]] [col("__TIDYPOLARS_TEMP_SORT__1")] #>          WITH_COLUMNS: #>          [col("drat").alias("__TIDYPOLARS_TEMP_SORT__1")]  #>           DF ["mpg", "cyl", "disp", "hp", ...]; PROJECT */11 COLUMNS  # optimized query run by polars cat(explain(query)) #> simple ПҖ 1/1 ["mpg"] #>   SORT BY [nulls_last: [true]] [col("__TIDYPOLARS_TEMP_SORT__1")] #>      WITH_COLUMNS: #>      [col("drat").alias("__TIDYPOLARS_TEMP_SORT__1")]  #>       FILTER [(col("cyl")) == (3.0)] #>       FROM #>         DF ["mpg", "cyl", "disp", "hp", ...]; PROJECT["mpg", "cyl", "drat"] 3/11 COLUMNS

what: Fetch n rows of a LazyFrame вҖ” fetch
title: Fetch n rows of a LazyFrame вҖ” fetch
text: Use head() collect() get subset data.

title: Fetch n rows of a LazyFrame вҖ” fetch
code: fetch(   .data,   n_rows = 500,   type_coercion = TRUE,   predicate_pushdown = TRUE,   projection_pushdown = TRUE,   simplify_expression = TRUE,   slice_pushdown = TRUE,   comm_subplan_elim = TRUE,   comm_subexpr_elim = TRUE,   cluster_with_columns = TRUE,   no_optimization = FALSE,   engine = c("auto", "in-memory", "streaming"),   streaming = FALSE )

title: Fetch n rows of a LazyFrame вҖ” fetch
text: .data Polars LazyFrame n_rows Number rows fetch. type_coercion Coerce types operations succeed run minimal required memory (default TRUE). predicate_pushdown Applies filters early possible scan level (default TRUE). projection_pushdown Select columns needed scan level (default TRUE). simplify_expression Various optimizations, constant folding replacing expensive operations faster alternatives (default TRUE). slice_pushdown load required slice scan. materialize sliced outputs level. materialize sliced outputs (default TRUE). comm_subplan_elim Cache branching subplans occur self-joins unions (default TRUE). comm_subexpr_elim Cache common subexpressions (default TRUE). cluster_with_columns Combine sequential independent calls $with_columns(). no_optimization Sets following optimizations FALSE: predicate_pushdown, projection_pushdown,  slice_pushdown, simplify_expression. Default FALSE. engine engine name use processing query. One followings: "auto" (default): Select engine automatically. "-memory" engine selected cases. "-memory": Use -memory engine. "streaming": Use streaming engine, usually faster can handle larger--memory data. streaming Deprecated, use engine instead.

title: Fetch n rows of a LazyFrame вҖ” fetch
text: parameter n_rows indicates many rows LazyFrame used beginning query, guarantee n_rows returned. example, query contains filter join operations datasets, final number rows can lower n_rows. hand, appending rows query can lead output rows n_rows.

what: Fill in missing values with previous or next value вҖ” fill.polars_data_frame
title: Fill in missing values with previous or next value вҖ” fill.polars_data_frame
text: Fills missing values selected columns using next previous entry. useful common output format values repeated, recorded change.

title: Fill in missing values with previous or next value вҖ” fill.polars_data_frame
code: # S3 method for class 'polars_data_frame' fill(data, ..., .by = NULL, .direction = c("down", "up", "downup", "updown"))

title: Fill in missing values with previous or next value вҖ” fill.polars_data_frame
text: data Polars Data/LazyFrame ... expression accepted dplyr::select(): variable names, column numbers, select helpers, etc. .Optionally, selection columns group just operation, functioning alternative group_by(). group order maintained, use group_by() want control . .direction Direction fill missing values. Either "" (default), "", "downup" (.e. first ) "updown" (first ).

title: Fill in missing values with previous or next value вҖ” fill.polars_data_frame
text: grouped Data/LazyFrames, fill() applied within group, meaning fill across group boundaries.

title: Fill in missing values with previous or next value вҖ” fill.polars_data_frame
code: pl_test <- polars::pl$DataFrame(x = c(NA, 1), y = c(2, NA))  fill(pl_test, everything(), .direction = "down") #> shape: (2, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x    в”Ҷ y   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ ---  в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64  в”Ҷ f64 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ null в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1.0  в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ fill(pl_test, everything(), .direction = "up") #> shape: (2, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64  в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 2.0  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # with grouped data, it doesn't use values from other groups pl_grouped <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   grp = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 3),   x = c(1, NA, NA, NA, 2, NA),   y = c(3, NA, 4, NA, 3, 1) ) |>   group_by(grp)  fill(pl_grouped, x, y, .direction = "down") #> shape: (6, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ grp в”Ҷ x    в”Ҷ y    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ f64  в”Ҷ f64  в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ A   в”Ҷ 1.0  в”Ҷ 3.0  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ A   в”Ҷ 1.0  в”Ҷ 3.0  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ A   в”Ҷ 1.0  в”Ҷ 4.0  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ B   в”Ҷ null в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ B   в”Ҷ 2.0  в”Ҷ 3.0  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ B   в”Ҷ 2.0  в”Ҷ 1.0  в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ #> Groups [2]: grp #> Maintain order: FALSE

what: Keep or drop rows that match a condition вҖ” filter.polars_data_frame
title: Keep or drop rows that match a condition вҖ” filter.polars_data_frame
text: functions used subset data frame, applying expressions ... determine rows kept (filter()) dropped (filter_out()). Multiple conditions can supplied separated comma. combined & operator. filter() filter_out() treat NA like FALSE. subtle behavior can impact write conditions missing values involved. See section Missing values important details examples. filter_out() available dplyr >= 1.2.0.

title: Keep or drop rows that match a condition вҖ” filter.polars_data_frame
code: # S3 method for class 'polars_data_frame' filter(.data, ..., .by = NULL)  # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' filter(.data, ..., .by = NULL)  # S3 method for class 'polars_data_frame' filter_out(.data, ..., .by = NULL)  # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' filter_out(.data, ..., .by = NULL)

title: Keep or drop rows that match a condition вҖ” filter.polars_data_frame
text: .data Polars Data/LazyFrame ... Expressions return logical value, defined terms variables data. multiple expressions included, combined & operator. rows conditions evaluate TRUE kept (filter()) dropped (filter_out()). .Optionally, selection columns group just operation, functioning alternative group_by(). group order maintained, use group_by() want control .

title: Keep or drop rows that match a condition вҖ” filter.polars_data_frame
text: Read section dplyr documentation.

text: read_csv_polars() imports data Polars DataFrame. scan_csv_polars() imports data Polars LazyFrame.

title: Import data from CSV file(s) вҖ” from_csv
code: read_csv_polars(   source,   ...,   has_header = TRUE,   separator = ",",   comment_prefix = NULL,   quote_char = "\"",   skip_rows = 0,   schema = NULL,   schema_overrides = NULL,   null_values = NULL,   ignore_errors = FALSE,   cache = FALSE,   infer_schema_length = 100,   n_rows = NULL,   encoding = "utf8",   low_memory = FALSE,   rechunk = TRUE,   skip_rows_after_header = 0,   row_index_name = NULL,   row_index_offset = 0,   try_parse_dates = FALSE,   eol_char = "\n",   raise_if_empty = TRUE,   truncate_ragged_lines = FALSE,   include_file_paths = NULL,   dtypes,   reuse_downloaded )  scan_csv_polars(   source,   ...,   has_header = TRUE,   separator = ",",   comment_prefix = NULL,   quote_char = "\"",   skip_rows = 0,   schema = NULL,   schema_overrides = NULL,   null_values = NULL,   ignore_errors = FALSE,   cache = FALSE,   infer_schema_length = 100,   n_rows = NULL,   encoding = "utf8",   low_memory = FALSE,   rechunk = TRUE,   skip_rows_after_header = 0,   row_index_name = NULL,   row_index_offset = 0,   try_parse_dates = FALSE,   eol_char = "\n",   raise_if_empty = TRUE,   truncate_ragged_lines = FALSE,   include_file_paths = NULL,   dtypes,   reuse_downloaded )

title: Import data from CSV file(s) вҖ” from_csv
text:
source Path(s) file directory. needing authenticate scanning cloud locations, see storage_options parameter. ... dots future extensions must empty. has_header Indicate first row dataset header .FALSE, column names autogenerated following format: "column_x" x enumeration every column dataset starting 1. separator Single byte character use separator file. comment_prefix string, can 5 symbols length, used indicate start comment line. instance, can set # //. quote_char Single byte character used quoting. Set NULL turn special handling escaping quotes. skip_rows Start reading particular number rows. header parsed offset. schema Provide schema. means polars schema inference. argument expects complete schema, whereas schema_overrides can used partially overwrite schema. must list. Names list elements used match inferred columns. schema_overrides Overwrite dtypes inference. must list. Names list elements used match inferred columns. null_values Character vector specifying values interpret NA values. can named, case names specify columns replacement must made (e.g. c(col1 = "")). ignore_errors Keep reading file even lines yield errors. can also use infer_schema = FALSE read columns UTF8 check values might cause issue. cache Cache result reading. infer_schema_length maximum number rows scan schema inference. NULL, full data may scanned (slow). Set infer_schema = FALSE read columns pl$String. n_rows Stop reading source reading n_rows. encoding Either "utf8" "utf8-lossy". Lossy means invalid UTF8 values replaced "?" characters. low_memory Reduce memory pressure expense performance. rechunk Reallocate contiguous memory chunks/files parsed. skip_rows_after_header Skip number rows header parsed. row_index_name NULL, insert row index column given name. row_index_offset Offset start row index column (used name set row_index_name). try_parse_dates Try automatically parse dates. ISO8601-like formats can inferred, well handful others. succeed, column remains data type pl$String. eol_char Single byte end line character (default: "
"). encountering file Windows line endings ("
"), one can go default "
". extra "" removed processed. raise_if_empty FALSE, parsing empty file returns empty DataFrame LazyFrame. truncate_ragged_lines Truncate lines longer schema. include_file_paths Include path source file(s) column name. dtypes Deprecated, use schema_overrides instead. reuse_downloaded Deprecated replacement.

title: Import data from CSV file(s) вҖ” from_csv
text: scan function returns LazyFrame, read function returns DataFrame.

title: Import data from CSV file(s) вҖ” from_csv
code: ### Read or scan a single CSV file ------------------------  # Setup: create a CSV file dest <- withr::local_tempfile(fileext = ".csv") write.csv(mtcars, dest, row.names = FALSE)  # Import this file as a DataFrame for eager evaluation read_csv_polars(dest) |>   arrange(mpg) #> shape: (32, 11) #> disp  в”Ҷ hp  в”Ҷ вҖҰ в”Ҷ vs  в”Ҷ am  в”Ҷ gear в”Ҷ carb в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ ---  в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---   в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ   в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64  в”Ҷ i64 в”Ҷ f64   в”Ҷ i64 в”Ҷ   в”Ҷ i64 в”Ҷ i64 в”Ҷ i64  в”Ҷ i64  в”Ӯ #> i64 в”Ҷ i64  в”Ҷ i64  в”Ӯ #> compute() #> shape: (32, 11) #> i64 в”Ҷ str  в”Ҷ f32  в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   ### Read or scan several all CSV files in a folder ------------------------  # Setup: create a folder "output" that contains two CSV files dest_folder <- withr::local_tempdir(tmpdir = "output") dir.create(dest_folder, showWarnings = FALSE) dest1 <- file.path(dest_folder, "output_1.csv") dest2 <- file.path(dest_folder, "output_2.csv")  write.csv(mtcars[1:16, ], dest1, row.names = FALSE) write.csv(mtcars[17:32, ], dest2, row.names = FALSE) list.files(dest_folder) #> [1] "output_1.csv" "output_2.csv"  # Import all files as a LazyFrame scan_csv_polars(dest_folder) |>   arrange(mpg) |>   compute() #> shape: (32, 11) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ mpg  в”Ҷ   ### Read or scan all CSV files that match a glob pattern ------------------------  # Setup: create a folder "output_glob" that contains three CSV files, # two of which follow the pattern "output_XXX.csv" dest_folder <- withr::local_tempdir(tmpdir = "output_glob") dir.create(dest_folder, showWarnings = FALSE) dest1 <- file.path(dest_folder, "output_1.csv") dest2 <- file.path(dest_folder, "output_2.csv") dest3 <- file.path(dest_folder, "other_output.csv")  write.csv(mtcars[1:16, ], dest1, row.names = FALSE) write.csv(mtcars[17:32, ], dest2, row.names = FALSE) write.csv(iris, dest3, row.names = FALSE) list.files(dest_folder) #> [1] "other_output.csv" "output_1.csv"     "output_2.csv"      # Import only the files whose name match "output_XXX.csv" as a LazyFrame scan_csv_polars(paste0(dest_folder, "/output_*.csv")) |>   arrange(mpg) |>   compute() #> shape: (32, 11) #> what: Import data from IPC file(s) вҖ” from_ipc
title: Import data from IPC file(s) вҖ” from_ipc
text: read_ipc_polars() imports data Polars DataFrame. scan_ipc_polars() imports data Polars LazyFrame.

title: Import data from IPC file(s) вҖ” from_ipc
code: read_ipc_polars(   source,   ...,   n_rows = NULL,   row_index_name = NULL,   row_index_offset = 0L,   rechunk = FALSE,   cache = TRUE,   include_file_paths = NULL,   memory_map )  scan_ipc_polars(   source,   ...,   n_rows = NULL,   row_index_name = NULL,   row_index_offset = 0L,   rechunk = FALSE,   cache = TRUE,   include_file_paths = NULL,   memory_map )

title: Import data from IPC file(s) вҖ” from_ipc
text: source Path(s) file directory. needing authenticate scanning cloud locations, see storage_options parameter. ... dots future extensions must empty. n_rows Stop reading source reading n_rows. row_index_name NULL, insert row index column given name. row_index_offset Offset start row index column (used name set row_index_name). rechunk Reallocate contiguous memory chunks/files parsed. cache Cache result reading. include_file_paths Include path source file(s) column name. memory_map Deprecated replacement.

title: Import data from IPC file(s) вҖ” from_ipc
text: scan function returns LazyFrame, read function returns DataFrame.

title: Import data from IPC file(s) вҖ” from_ipc

all IPC files that match a glob pattern ------------------------  # Setup: create a folder "output_glob" that contains three IPC files, # two of which follow the pattern "output_XXX.ipc" dest_folder <- withr::local_tempdir(tmpdir = "output_glob") dir.create(dest_folder, showWarnings = FALSE) dest1 <- file.path(dest_folder, "output_1.ipc") dest2 <- file.path(dest_folder, "output_2.ipc") dest3 <- file.path(dest_folder, "other_output.ipc")  arrow::write_ipc_file(mtcars[1:16, ], dest1) arrow::write_ipc_file(mtcars[17:32, ], dest2) arrow::write_ipc_file(iris, dest3) list.files(dest_folder) #> [1] "other_output.ipc" "output_1.ipc"     "output_2.ipc"      # Import only the files whose name match "output_XXX.ipc" as a LazyFrame scan_ipc_polars(paste0(dest_folder, "/output_*.ipc")) |>   arrange(mpg) |>   compute() #> shape: (32, 11) #> what: Import data from NDJSON file(s) вҖ” from_ndjson
title: Import data from NDJSON file(s) вҖ” from_ndjson
text: read_ndjson_polars() imports data Polars DataFrame. scan_ndjson_polars() imports data Polars LazyFrame.

title: Import data from NDJSON file(s) вҖ” from_ndjson
code: read_ndjson_polars(   source,   ...,   infer_schema_length = 100,   batch_size = NULL,   n_rows = NULL,   low_memory = FALSE,   rechunk = FALSE,   row_index_name = NULL,   row_index_offset = 0,   ignore_errors = FALSE,   reuse_downloaded )  scan_ndjson_polars(   source,   ...,   infer_schema_length = 100,   batch_size = NULL,   n_rows = NULL,   low_memory = FALSE,   rechunk = FALSE,   row_index_name = NULL,   row_index_offset = 0,   ignore_errors = FALSE,   reuse_downloaded )

title: Import data from NDJSON file(s) вҖ” from_ndjson
text: source Path(s) file directory. needing authenticate scanning cloud locations, see storage_options parameter. ... dots future extensions must empty. infer_schema_length maximum number rows scan schema inference. NULL, full data may scanned (slow). Set infer_schema = FALSE read columns pl$String. batch_size Number rows read batch. n_rows Stop reading source reading n_rows. low_memory Reduce memory pressure expense performance. rechunk Reallocate contiguous memory chunks/files parsed. row_index_name NULL, insert row index column given name. row_index_offset Offset start row index column (used name set row_index_name). ignore_errors Keep reading file even lines yield errors. can also use infer_schema = FALSE read columns UTF8 check values might cause issue. reuse_downloaded Deprecated replacement.

title: Import data from NDJSON file(s) вҖ” from_ndjson
text: scan function returns LazyFrame, read function returns DataFrame.

title: Import data from NDJSON file(s) вҖ” from_ndjson
code: ### Read or scan a single NDJSON file ------------------------  # Setup: create a NDJSON file dest <- withr::local_tempfile(fileext = ".json") jsonlite::stream_out(mtcars, file(dest), verbose = FALSE)  # Import this file as a DataFrame for eager evaluation read_ndjson_polars(dest) |>   arrange(mpg) #> shape: (32, 12) #>   ### Read or scan all NDJSON files that match a glob pattern ------------------------  # Setup: create a folder "output_glob" that contains three NDJSON files, # two of which follow the pattern "output_XXX.json" dest_folder <- withr::local_tempdir(tmpdir = "output_glob") dir.create(dest_folder, showWarnings = FALSE) dest1 <- file.path(dest_folder, "output_1.json") dest2 <- file.path(dest_folder, "output_2.json") dest3 <- file.path(dest_folder, "other_output.json")  jsonlite::stream_out(mtcars[1:16, ], file(dest1), verbose = FALSE) jsonlite::stream_out(mtcars[17:32, ], file(dest2), verbose = FALSE) jsonlite::stream_out(iris, file(dest3), verbose = FALSE) list.files(dest_folder) #> [1] "other_output.json" "output_1.json"     "output_2.json"      # Import only the files whose name match "output_XXX.json" as a LazyFrame scan_ndjson_polars(paste0(dest_folder, "/output_*.json")) |>   arrange(mpg) |>   compute() #> shape: (32, 12) #> what: Import data from Parquet file(s) вҖ” from_parquet
title: Import data from Parquet file(s) вҖ” from_parquet
text: read_parquet_polars() imports data Polars DataFrame. scan_parquet_polars() imports data Polars LazyFrame.

title: Import data from Parquet file(s) вҖ” from_parquet
code: read_parquet_polars(   source,   ...,   n_rows = NULL,   row_index_name = NULL,   row_index_offset = 0L,   parallel = "auto",   hive_partitioning = NULL,   hive_schema = NULL,   try_parse_hive_dates = TRUE,   glob = TRUE,   rechunk = TRUE,   low_memory = FALSE,   storage_options = NULL,   use_statistics = TRUE,   cache = TRUE,   include_file_paths = NULL )  scan_parquet_polars(   source,   ...,   n_rows = NULL,   row_index_name = NULL,   row_index_offset = 0L,   parallel = "auto",   hive_partitioning = NULL,   hive_schema = NULL,   try_parse_hive_dates = TRUE,   glob = TRUE,   rechunk = FALSE,   low_memory = FALSE,   storage_options = NULL,   use_statistics = TRUE,   cache = TRUE,   include_file_paths = NULL )

title: Import data from Parquet file(s) вҖ” from_parquet
text: source Path(s) file directory. needing authenticate scanning cloud locations, see storage_options parameter. ... dots future extensions must empty. n_rows Stop reading source reading n_rows. row_index_name NULL, insert row index column given name. row_index_offset Offset start row index column (used name set row_index_name). parallel determines direction strategy parallelism. "auto" (default): try determine optimal direction. "prefiltered":  Strategy first evaluates pushed-predicates parallel determines mask rows read. , parallelizes columns row groups filtering rows need read. can provide significant speedups large files (.e. many row-groups) predicate filters clustered rows filters heavily. cases, prefiltered may slow scan compared strategies. Falls back "auto" predicate given. "columns", "row_groups": Use specified direction. "none": parallelism. hive_partitioning Infer statistics schema Hive partitioned sources use prune reads. NULL (default), automatically enabled single directory passed, otherwise disabled. hive_schema list containing column names data types columns data partitioned, e.g. list(= pl$String, b = pl$Float32). NULL (default), schema Hive partitions inferred. try_parse_hive_dates Whether try parsing hive values date / datetime types. glob Expand path given via globbing rules. rechunk Reallocate contiguous memory chunks/files parsed. low_memory Reduce memory pressure expense performance storage_options Named vector containing options indicate connect cloud provider. cloud providers currently supported AWS, GCP, Azure. See supported keys : aws gcp azure Hugging Face (hf://): Accepts API key token parameter c(token = YOUR_TOKEN) setting HF_TOKEN environment variable. storage_options provided, Polars try infer information environment variables. use_statistics Use statistics parquet determine pages can skipped reading. cache Cache result reading. include_file_paths Include path source file(s) column name.

title: Import data from Parquet file(s) вҖ” from_parquet
text: scan function returns LazyFrame, read function returns DataFrame.

title: Import data from Parquet file(s) вҖ” from_parquet
code: ### Read or scan a single Parquet file ------------------------  # Setup: create a Parquet file dest <- withr::local_tempfile(fileext = ".parquet") dat <- as_polars_df(mtcars) write_parquet_polars(dat, dest)  # Import this file as a DataFrame for eager evaluation read_parquet_polars(dest) |>   arrange(mpg) #> shape: (32, 11) #>   ### Read or scan all Parquet files that match a glob pattern ------------------------  # Setup: create a folder "output_glob" that contains three Parquet files, # two of which follow the pattern "output_XXX.parquet" dest_folder <- withr::local_tempdir(tmpdir = "output_glob") dir.create(dest_folder, showWarnings = FALSE) dest1 <- file.path(dest_folder, "output_1.parquet") dest2 <- file.path(dest_folder, "output_2.parquet") dest3 <- file.path(dest_folder, "other_output.parquet")  write_parquet_polars(as_polars_df(mtcars[1:16, ]), dest1) write_parquet_polars(as_polars_df(mtcars[17:32, ]), dest2) write_parquet_polars(as_polars_df(iris), dest3) list.files(dest_folder) #> [1] "other_output.parquet" "output_1.parquet" title: Group by one or more variables вҖ” group_by.polars_data_frame
text: data operations done groups defined variables. group_by() takes existing Polars Data/LazyFrame converts grouped one operations performed "group". ungroup() removes grouping.

title: Group by one or more variables вҖ” group_by.polars_data_frame
code: # S3 method for class 'polars_data_frame' group_by(.data, ..., maintain_order = FALSE, .add = FALSE, .drop = TRUE)  # S3 method for class 'polars_data_frame' ungroup(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' group_by(.data, ..., maintain_order = FALSE, .add = FALSE, .drop = TRUE)  # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' ungroup(x, ...)

title: Group by one or more variables вҖ” group_by.polars_data_frame
text: .data Polars Data/LazyFrame ... Variables group (used group_by() ). used ungroup(). maintain_order Maintain row order. performance reasons, FALSE default). Setting TRUE can slow process large datasets prevents use streaming. .add FALSE (default), group_by() override existing groups. add existing groups, use .add = TRUE. .drop Unsupported. present provide good error message specified user. x Polars Data/LazyFrame

title: Mutating joins вҖ” left_join.polars_data_frame
text: x, y Two Polars Data/LazyFrames Variables join . NULL (default), *_join() perform natural join, using variables common across x y. message lists variables can check correct; suppress message supplying explicitly. can take character vector, like c("x", "y") x y datasets. join variables name, use equalities character vector, like c("x1" = "x2", "y"). use character vector, join can done using strict equality. can also specification created dplyr::join_by(). Contrary input character vector shown , join_by() uses unquoted column names, e.g join_by(x1 == x2, y). Finally, inner_join() also supports inequality joins, e.g. join_by(x1 >= x2), helpers (), overlaps(), within(). See documentation dplyr::join_by() information. join types likely support inequality joins future. copy, keep supported. suffix non-joined duplicate variables x y, suffixes added output disambiguate . character vector length 2. ... Dots empty. na_matches two NA values match? "na", default, treats two NA values equal. "never" treats two NA values different never match together values. Note joining Polars Data/LazyFrames, NaN always considered equal, matter value na_matches. differs original dplyr implementation. relationship Handling expected relationship keys x y. Must one following: NULL, default, equivalent "many--many". expect relationship x y. "one--one" expects row x match 1 row y row y match 1 row x. "one--many" expects row y match 1 row x. "many--one" expects row x matches 1 row y.

code: test <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   x = c(1, 2, 3),   y1 = c(1, 2, 3),   z = c(1, 2, 3) )  test2 <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   x = c(1, 2, 4),   y2 = c(1, 2, 4),   z2 = c(4, 5, 7) )  test #> shape: (3, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y1  в”Ҷ z   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  test2 #> shape: (3, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y2  в”Ҷ z2  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 4.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 5.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 4.0 в”Ҷ 4.0 в”Ҷ 7.0 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # default is to use common columns, here "x" only left_join(test, test2) #> Joining by `x` #> shape: (3, 5) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y1  в”Ҷ z   в”Ҷ y2   в”Ҷ z2   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64  в”Ҷ f64  в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0  в”Ҷ 4.0  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0  в”Ҷ 5.0  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ null в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # we can specify the columns on which to join with join_by()... left_join(test, test2, by = join_by(x, y1 == y2)) #> shape: (3, 4) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y1  в”Ҷ z   в”Ҷ z2   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64  в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 4.0  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 5.0  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # ... or with a character vector left_join(test, test2, by = c("x", "y1" = "y2")) #> shape: (3, 4) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y1  в”Ҷ z   в”Ҷ z2   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64  в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 4.0  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 5.0  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # we can customize the suffix of common column names not used to join test2 <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   x = c(1, 2, 4),   y1 = c(1, 2, 4),   z = c(4, 5, 7) )  left_join(test, test2, by = "x", suffix = c("_left", "_right")) #> shape: (3, 5) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y1_left в”Ҷ z_left в”Ҷ y1_right в”Ҷ z_right в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---     в”Ҷ ---    в”Ҷ ---      в”Ҷ ---     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64     в”Ҷ f64    в”Ҷ f64      в”Ҷ f64     в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0     в”Ҷ 1.0    в”Ҷ 1.0      в”Ҷ 4.0     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0     в”Ҷ 2.0    в”Ҷ 2.0      в”Ҷ 5.0     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3.0 в”Ҷ 3.0     в”Ҷ 3.0    в”Ҷ null     в”Ҷ null    в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # the argument "relationship" ensures the join matches the expectation country <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   iso = c("FRA", "DEU"),   value = 1:2 ) country #> shape: (2, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ iso в”Ҷ value в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ i32   в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ FRA в”Ҷ 1     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ DEU в”Ҷ 2     в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  country_year <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   iso = rep(c("FRA", "DEU"), each = 2),   year = rep(2019:2020, 2),   value2 = 3:6 ) country_year #> shape: (4, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ iso в”Ҷ year в”Ҷ value2 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ҷ ---    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ i32  в”Ҷ i32    в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ FRA в”Ҷ 2019 в”Ҷ 3      в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ FRA в”Ҷ 2020 в”Ҷ 4      в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ DEU в”Ҷ 2019 в”Ҷ 5      в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ DEU в”Ҷ 2020 в”Ҷ 6      в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # We expect that each row in "x" matches only one row in "y" but, it's not # true as each row of "x" matches two rows of "y" tryCatch(   left_join(country, country_year, join_by(iso), relationship = "one-to-one"),   error = function(e) e ) #> <error/rlang_error> #> Error in `x$join()`: #> ! what: Helper functions to export data as a partitioned output вҖ” partitioned_output
title: Helper functions to export data as a partitioned output вҖ” partitioned_output
text: Partitioning schemes used write multiple files sink_*() write_*_polars() functions. partition_by(): Configuration writing multiple output files. Supports partitioning key, file size limits, . following functions deprecated removed future release: partition_by_key(): use partition_by(key = ...) instead. partition_by_max_size(): use partition_by(max_rows_per_file = ...) instead.

title: Helper functions to export data as a partitioned output вҖ” partitioned_output
code: partition_by(   base_path,   ...,   key = NULL,   include_key = NULL,   max_rows_per_file = NULL,   approximate_bytes_per_file = NULL )  partition_by_key(base_path, ..., by, include_key = TRUE)  partition_by_max_size(base_path, ..., max_size)

title: Helper functions to export data as a partitioned output вҖ” partitioned_output
text: base_path base path output files. Use mkdir option sink_* write_*_polars() functions ensure directories path created. ... dots future extensions must empty. key Something can coerced list Polars expressions. Used partition . include_key bool indicating whether include key columns output files. Can used key specified, otherwise NULL. max_rows_per_file integer-ish value indicating maximum size rows generated files. approximate_bytes_per_file integer-ish value indicating approximate number bytes write file, NULL. measured estimated size DataFrame memory. Defaults approximately 4GB key specified without max_rows_per_file, otherwise unlimited. Something can coerced list Polars expressions. Used partition . Use key property partition_by() instead. max_size integer-ish value indicating maximum size rows generated files. Use max_rows_per_file property partition_by() instead.

title: Pivot a Data/LazyFrame from wide to long вҖ” pivot_longer.polars_data_frame
text: data Polars Data/LazyFrame cols Columns pivot longer format. Can anything accepted dplyr::select(). ... Dots empty. names_to (quoted) name column contain column names specified cols. names_prefix regular expression used remove matching text start variable name. values_to string specifying name column create data stored cell values.

title: Pivot a Data/LazyFrame from long to wide вҖ” pivot_wider.polars_data_frame
text: data Polars Data/LazyFrame. ... Dots empty. id_cols set columns uniquely identify observation. Typically used redundant variables, .e. variables whose values perfectly correlated existing variables. Defaults columns data except columns specified names_from values_from. tidyselect expression supplied, evaluated data removing columns specified names_from values_from. names_from (quoted unquoted) column names whose values used names new columns. values_from (quoted unquoted) column names whose values used fill new columns. names_prefix String added start every variable name. particularly useful names_from numeric vector want create syntactic variable names. names_sep names_from values_from contains multiple variables, used join values together single string use column name. names_glue Instead names_sep names_prefix, can supply glue specification uses names_from columns create custom column names. values_fill scalar used replace missing values new columns. Note type value applied new columns. example, provide character value fill numeric columns, columns converted character.

title: Pivot a Data/LazyFrame from long to wide вҖ” pivot_wider.polars_data_frame
text: Arguments supported original implementation tidyverse listed throw warning default specified. change behavior error instead, use options(tidypolars_unknown_args = "error").

title: Change column order вҖ” relocate.polars_data_frame
text: Use relocate() change column positions, using syntax select() make easy move blocks columns .

title: Change column order вҖ” relocate.polars_data_frame
code: # S3 method for class 'polars_data_frame' relocate(.data, ..., .before = NULL, .after = NULL)  # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' relocate(.data, ..., .before = NULL, .after = NULL)

title: Change column order вҖ” relocate.polars_data_frame
text: .data Polars Data/LazyFrame ... expression accepted dplyr::select(): variable names, column numbers, select helpers, etc. ., .Column name (either quoted unquoted) indicates destination columns selected .... Supplying neither move columns left-hand side; specifying error.

title: Rename columns вҖ” rename.polars_data_frame
text: Rename columns

title: Rename columns вҖ” rename.polars_data_frame
code: # S3 method for class 'polars_data_frame' rename(.data, ...)  # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' rename(.data, ...)  # S3 method for class 'polars_data_frame' rename_with(.data, .fn, .cols = tidyselect::everything(), ...)  # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' rename_with(.data, .fn, .cols = tidyselect::everything(), ...)

title: Rename columns вҖ” rename.polars_data_frame
text: .data Polars Data/LazyFrame ... rename(), use new_name = old_name rename selected variables. also possible use quotation marks, e.g "new_name" = "old_name". rename_with, additional arguments passed fn. .fn Function apply column names .cols Columns apply fn. Can anything accepted dplyr::select().

what: Replace NAs with specified values вҖ” replace_na.polars_data_frame
title: Replace NAs with specified values вҖ” replace_na.polars_data_frame
text: Replace NAs specified values

title: Replace NAs with specified values вҖ” replace_na.polars_data_frame
code: # S3 method for class 'polars_data_frame' replace_na(data, replace, ...)  # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' replace_na(data, replace, ...)

title: Replace NAs with specified values вҖ” replace_na.polars_data_frame
text: data Polars Data/LazyFrame replace Either scalar used replace NA columns, named list column name value used replace NA . ... Dots empty.

title: Replace NAs with specified values вҖ” replace_na.polars_data_frame
code: pl_test <- polars::pl$DataFrame(x = c(NA, 1), y = c(2, NA))  replace_na(pl_test, list(x = 0, y = 999)) #> shape: (2, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64   в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 0.0 в”Ҷ 2.0   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 999.0 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

what: Group input by rows вҖ” rowwise.polars_data_frame
title: Group input by rows вҖ” rowwise.polars_data_frame
text: [EXPERIMENTAL] rowwise() allows compute Data/LazyFrame row---time. useful vectorised function exist. rowwise() produces another type grouped data, therefore can removed ungroup().

title: Group input by rows вҖ” rowwise.polars_data_frame
code: # S3 method for class 'polars_data_frame' rowwise(data, ...)  # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' rowwise(data, ...)

title: Group input by rows вҖ” rowwise.polars_data_frame
text: data Polars Data/LazyFrame ... expression accepted dplyr::select(): variable names, column numbers, select helpers, etc.

title: Group input by rows вҖ” rowwise.polars_data_frame
text: Polars Data/LazyFrame.

title: Group input by rows вҖ” rowwise.polars_data_frame
code: df <- polars::pl$DataFrame(x = c(1, 3, 4), y = c(2, 1, 5), z = c(2, 3, 1))  # Compute the mean of x, y, z in each row df |>  rowwise() |>  mutate(m = mean(c(x, y, z))) #> shape: (3, 4) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ z   в”Ҷ m        в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---      в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64      в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 1.666667 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ 2.333333 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 4.0 в”Ҷ 5.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 3.333333 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ #>  #> Rowwise: TRUE  # Compute the min and max of x and y in each row df |>  rowwise() |>  mutate(min = min(c(x, y)), max = max(c(x, y))) #> shape: (3, 5) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ z   в”Ҷ min в”Ҷ max в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 4.0 в”Ҷ 5.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 4.0 в”Ҷ 5.0 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ #>  #> Rowwise: TRUE

title: Filtering joins вҖ” semi_join.polars_data_frame
text: Filtering joins filter rows x based presence absence matches y: semi_join() return rows x match y. anti_join() return rows x without match y.

title: Filtering joins вҖ” semi_join.polars_data_frame
code: # S3 method for class 'polars_data_frame' semi_join(x, y, by = NULL, ..., na_matches = "na")  # S3 method for class 'polars_data_frame' anti_join(x, y, by = NULL, ..., na_matches = "na")  # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' semi_join(x, y, by = NULL, ..., na_matches = "na")  # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' anti_join(x, y, by = NULL, ..., na_matches = "na")

title: Filtering joins вҖ” semi_join.polars_data_frame
text: x, y Two Polars Data/LazyFrames Variables join . NULL (default), *_join() perform natural join, using variables common across x y. message lists variables can check correct; suppress message supplying explicitly. can take character vector, like c("x", "y") x y datasets. join variables name, use equalities character vector, like c("x1" = "x2", "y"). use character vector, join can done using strict equality. can also specification created dplyr::join_by(). Contrary input character vector shown , join_by() uses unquoted column names, e.g join_by(x1 == x2, y). Finally, inner_join() also supports inequality joins, e.g. join_by(x1 >= x2), helpers (), overlaps(), within(). See documentation dplyr::join_by() information. join types likely support inequality joins future. ... Dots empty. na_matches two NA values match? "na", default, treats two NA values equal. "never" treats two NA values different never match together values. Note joining Polars Data/LazyFrames, NaN always considered equal, matter value na_matches. differs original dplyr implementation.

title: Filtering joins вҖ” semi_join.polars_data_frame
text: Arguments supported original implementation tidyverse listed throw warning default specified. change behavior error instead, use options(tidypolars_unknown_args = "error").

title: Filtering joins вҖ” semi_join.polars_data_frame
code: test <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   x = c(1, 2, 3),   y = c(1, 2, 3),   z = c(1, 2, 3) )  test2 <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   x = c(1, 2, 4),   y = c(1, 2, 4),   z2 = c(1, 2, 4) )  test #> shape: (3, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ z   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  test2 #> shape: (3, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ z2  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 4.0 в”Ҷ 4.0 в”Ҷ 4.0 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # only keep the rows of `test` that have matching keys in `test2` semi_join(test, test2, by = c("x", "y")) #> shape: (2, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ z   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # only keep the rows of `test` that don't have matching keys in `test2` anti_join(test, test2, by = c("x", "y")) #> shape: (1, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ z   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 3.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

title: Separate a character column into multiple columns based on a substring вҖ” separate.polars_data_frame
text: Currently, splitting column position possible.

title: Separate a character column into multiple columns based on a substring вҖ” separate.polars_data_frame
code: # S3 method for class 'polars_data_frame' separate(data, col, into, sep = " ", remove = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'polars_lazy_frame' separate(data, col, into, sep = " ", remove = TRUE, ...)

title: Separate a character column into multiple columns based on a substring вҖ” separate.polars_data_frame
text: data Polars Data/LazyFrame col Column split Character vector containing names new variables create. Use NA omit variable output. sep regular expression used split column. remove TRUE, remove input column output data frame. ... Dots empty.

title: Separate a character column into multiple columns based on a substring вҖ” separate.polars_data_frame
code: # Split at each dot test <- polars::pl$DataFrame(x = c(NA, "x.y", "x.z", "y.z")) separate(test, x, into = c("foo", "foo2"), sep = "\\.") #> shape: (4, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ foo  в”Ҷ foo2 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ ---  в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str  в”Ҷ str  в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ null в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ x    в”Ҷ y    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ x    в”Ҷ z    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ y    в”Ҷ z    в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # Split on any number of whitespace test <- polars::pl$DataFrame(x = c(NA, "x y", "x  y", "x y  z")) separate(test, x, into = c("foo", "foo2"), sep = "\\s+") #> shape: (4, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ foo  в”Ҷ foo2 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ ---  в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str  в”Ҷ str  в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ null в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ x    в”Ҷ y    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ x    в”Ҷ y    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ x    в”Ҷ y    в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

what: Split a string column into rows вҖ” separate_longer
title: Split a string column into rows вҖ” separate_longer
text: functions takes string splits multiple rows: separate_longer_delim_polars() splits delimiter. polars equivalent tidyr::separate_longer_delim(). separate_longer_position_polars() splits fixed width. polars equivalent tidyr::separate_longer_position().

title: Split a string column into rows вҖ” separate_longer
