title: Split a string column into rows вҖ” separate_longer
code: library(polars) library(tidypolars)  # separate_longer_delim_polars: split by delimiter df <- pl$DataFrame(   id = 1:3,   x = c("a,b,c", "d,e", "f") ) separate_longer_delim_polars(df, x, delim = ",") #> shape: (6, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ x   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ str в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ a   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ b   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ c   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ d   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ e   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3   в”Ҷ f   в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # Multiple columns with broadcasting, the same as `tidyr` behavior df2 <- pl$DataFrame(   id = 1:2,   x = c("a,b", "c,d"),   y = c("1,2", "3,4") ) separate_longer_delim_polars(df2, c(x, y), delim = ",") #> shape: (4, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ str в”Ҷ str в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ a   в”Ҷ 1   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ b   в”Ҷ 2   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ c   в”Ҷ 3   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ d   в”Ҷ 4   в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # Multiple columns with broadcasting df3 <- pl$DataFrame(   id = 1:5,   x = c("a,b", NA, "", "c", ""),   y = c("1", "2,3", "4,5", NA, "") ) separate_longer_delim_polars(df3, c(x, y), delim = ",") #> shape: (8, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ x    в”Ҷ y    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ str  в”Ҷ str  в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ a    в”Ҷ 1    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ b    в”Ҷ 1    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ null в”Ҷ 2    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ null в”Ҷ 3    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3   в”Ҷ      в”Ҷ 4    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3   в”Ҷ      в”Ҷ 5    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 4   в”Ҷ c    в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 5   в”Ҷ      в”Ҷ      в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # separate_longer_position_polars: split by fixed width df4 <- pl$DataFrame(   id = 1:3,   x = c("abcd", "efg", "hi") ) separate_longer_position_polars(df4, x, width = 2) #> shape: (5, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ x   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ str в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ ab  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ cd  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ ef  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ g   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3   в”Ҷ hi  в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # keep_empty example: control whether empty strings are preserved df5 <- pl$DataFrame(   id = 1:4,   x = c("ab", "", "ef", NA) ) separate_longer_position_polars(df5, x, width = 2) #> shape: (3, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ x    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ str  в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ ab   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3   в”Ҷ ef   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 4   в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ separate_longer_position_polars(df5, x, width = 2, keep_empty = TRUE) #> shape: (4, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ x    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ str  в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ ab   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3   в”Ҷ ef   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 4   в”Ҷ null в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # Multiple columns with broadcasting df6 <- pl$DataFrame(   id = 1:3,   x = c("a", "bc", "def"),   y = c("12", "345", "67") ) # Shorter strings are recycled to match the longest in each row separate_longer_position_polars(df6, c(x, y), width = 2) #> shape: (5, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ str в”Ҷ str в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ a   в”Ҷ 12  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ bc  в”Ҷ 34  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ bc  в”Ҷ 5   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3   в”Ҷ de  в”Ҷ 67  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3   в”Ҷ f   в”Ҷ 67  в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

what: Stream output to a CSV file вҖ” sink_csv
title: Stream output to a CSV file вҖ” sink_csv
text: function allows stream LazyFrame larger RAM directly .csv file without collecting R session, thus preventing crashes small memory.

title: Stream output to a CSV file вҖ” sink_csv
code: sink_csv(   .data,   path,   ...,   include_bom = FALSE,   include_header = TRUE,   separator = ",",   line_terminator = "\n",   quote_char = "\"",   batch_size = 1024,   datetime_format = NULL,   date_format = NULL,   time_format = NULL,   float_precision = NULL,   null_value = "",   quote_style = "necessary",   maintain_order = TRUE,   type_coercion = TRUE,   predicate_pushdown = TRUE,   projection_pushdown = TRUE,   simplify_expression = TRUE,   slice_pushdown = TRUE,   no_optimization = FALSE,   mkdir = FALSE,   quote,   null_values )

title: Stream output to a CSV file вҖ” sink_csv
text: .data Polars LazyFrame. path Output file. Can also partition_*() function export output multiple files (see Details section ). ... Ignored. include_bom Whether include UTF-8 BOM (byte order mark) CSV output. include_header Whether include header CSV output. separator Separate CSV fields symbol. line_terminator String used end row. quote_char Byte use quoting character. batch_size Number rows processed per thread. datetime_format, date_format, time_format format string used format date time values. See ?strptime() accepted values. format specified, default fractional-second precision inferred maximum time unit found Datetime cols (). float_precision Number decimal places write, applied Float32 Float64 datatypes. null_value string representing null values (defaulting empty string). quote_style Determines quoting strategy used: "necessary" (default): puts quotes around fields necessary. necessary fields contain quote, delimiter record terminator. Quotes also necessary writing empty record (indistinguishable record one empty field). "always": puts quotes around every field. "non_numeric": puts quotes around fields non-numeric. Namely, writing field parse valid float integer, quotes used even strictly necessary. maintain_order Whether maintain order data processed (default TRUE). Setting FALSE slightly faster. type_coercion Coerce types operations succeed run minimal required memory (default TRUE). predicate_pushdown Applies filters early possible scan level (default TRUE). projection_pushdown Select columns needed scan level (default TRUE). simplify_expression Various optimizations, constant folding replacing expensive operations faster alternatives (default TRUE). slice_pushdown load required slice scan. materialize sliced outputs level. materialize sliced outputs (default TRUE). no_optimization Sets following optimizations FALSE: predicate_pushdown, projection_pushdown,  slice_pushdown, simplify_expression. Default FALSE. mkdir Recursively create directories path. quote Deprecated, use quote_char instead. null_values Deprecated, use null_value instead.

title: Stream output to a CSV file вҖ” sink_csv
text: possible export data multiple files based various parameters, values variables, file maximum number rows. See partition_by() details.

title: Stream output to a CSV file вҖ” sink_csv
code: # This is an example workflow where sink_csv() is not very useful because # the data would fit in memory. It simply is an example of using it at the # end of a piped workflow.  # Create files for the CSV input and output: file_csv <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") file_csv2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv")  # Write some data in a CSV file fake_data <- do.call("rbind", rep(list(mtcars), 1000)) write.csv(fake_data, file = file_csv, row.names = FALSE)  # In a new R session, we could read this file as a LazyFrame, do some operations, # and write it to another CSV file without ever collecting it in the R session: scan_csv_polars(file_csv) |>   filter(cyl %in% c(4, 6), mpg > 22) |>   mutate(     hp_gear_ratio = hp / gear   ) |>   sink_csv(path = file_csv2)   #---------------------------------------------- # Write a LazyFrame to multiple files depending on various strategies. my_lf <- as_polars_lf(mtcars)  # Split the LazyFrame by key(s) and write each split to a different file: out_path <- withr::local_tempdir() sink_csv(my_lf, partition_by_key(out_path, by = c("am", "cyl")), mkdir = TRUE) #> Warning: `partition_by_key()` was deprecated in tidypolars 0.16.0. #> в„№ Please use `partition_by(key = )` instead. fs::dir_tree(out_path) #> /tmp/RtmpcZqjs2/file19057ed2a6d #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ am=0.0 #> в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ cyl=4.0 #> в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.csv #> в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ cyl=6.0 #> в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.csv #> в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ cyl=8.0 #> в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.csv #> в””в”Җв”Җ am=1.0 #>     в”ңв”Җв”Җ cyl=4.0 #>     в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.csv #>     в”ңв”Җв”Җ cyl=6.0 #>     в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.csv #>     в””в”Җв”Җ cyl=8.0 #>         в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.csv  # Split the LazyFrame by max number of rows per file: out_path <- withr::local_tempdir() sink_csv(my_lf, partition_by_max_size(out_path, max_size = 5), mkdir = TRUE) #> Warning: `partition_by_max_size()` was deprecated in tidypolars 0.16.0. #> в„№ Please use `partition_by(max_rows_per_file = )` instead. fs::dir_tree(out_path) # mtcars has 32 rows so we have 7 output files #> /tmp/RtmpcZqjs2/file19053c28d23f #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ 00000000.csv #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ 00000001.csv #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ 00000002.csv #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ 00000003.csv #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ 00000004.csv #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ 00000005.csv #> в””в”Җв”Җ 00000006.csv

Ignored. compression NULL character compression method, "uncompressed" "lz4" "zstd". NULL equivalent "uncompressed". Choose "zstd" good compression performance. Choose "lz4" fast compression/decompression. compat_level Determines compatibility level exporting Polars' internal data structures. specifying new compatibility level, Polars exports internal data structures might interpretable Arrow implementations. level can specified name (e.g., "newest") scalar integer (currently, 0 1 supported). "newest" (default): Use highest level, currently 1 (Low compatibility). "oldest": 0 (High compatibility). maintain_order Whether maintain order data processed (default TRUE). Setting FALSE slightly faster. type_coercion Coerce types operations succeed run minimal required memory (default TRUE). predicate_pushdown Applies filters early possible scan level (default TRUE). projection_pushdown Select columns needed scan level (default TRUE). simplify_expression Various optimizations, constant folding replacing expensive operations faster alternatives (default TRUE). slice_pushdown load required slice scan. materialize sliced outputs level. materialize sliced outputs (default TRUE). no_optimization Sets following optimizations FALSE: predicate_pushdown, projection_pushdown,  slice_pushdown, simplify_expression. Default FALSE. mkdir Recursively create directories path.

It simply is an example of using it at the # end of a piped workflow.  # Create files for the IPC input and output: file_ipc <- tempfile(fileext = ".ipc") file_ipc2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".ipc")  # Write some data in an IPC file fake_data <- do.call("rbind", rep(list(mtcars), 1000)) arrow::write_ipc_file(fake_data, file_ipc)  # In a new R session, we could read this file as a LazyFrame, do some operations, # and write it to another IPC file without ever collecting it in the R session: scan_ipc_polars(file_ipc) |>   filter(cyl %in% c(4, 6), mpg > 22) |>   mutate(     hp_gear_ratio = hp / gear   ) |>   sink_ipc(path = file_ipc2)   #---------------------------------------------- # Write a LazyFrame to multiple files depending on various strategies. my_lf <- as_polars_lf(mtcars)  # Split the LazyFrame by key(s) and write each split to a different file: out_path <- withr::local_tempdir() sink_ipc(my_lf, partition_by_key(out_path, by = c("am", "cyl")), mkdir = TRUE) #> Warning: `partition_by_key()` was deprecated in tidypolars 0.16.0. #> в„№ Please use `partition_by(key = )` instead. fs::dir_tree(out_path) #> /tmp/RtmpcZqjs2/file1905180e4d5c #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ am=0.0 #> в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ cyl=4.0 #> в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.ipc #> в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ cyl=6.0 #> в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.ipc #> в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ cyl=8.0 #> в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.ipc #> в””в”Җв”Җ am=1.0 #>     в”ңв”Җв”Җ cyl=4.0 #>     в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.ipc #>     в”ңв”Җв”Җ cyl=6.0 #>     в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.ipc #>     в””в”Җв”Җ cyl=8.0 #>         в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.ipc  # Split the LazyFrame by max number of rows per file: out_path <- withr::local_tempdir() sink_ipc(my_lf, partition_by_max_size(out_path, max_size = 5), mkdir = TRUE) #> Warning: `partition_by_max_size()` was deprecated in tidypolars 0.16.0. #> в„№ Please use `partition_by(max_rows_per_file = )` instead. fs::dir_tree(out_path) # what: Stream output to a parquet file вҖ” sink_parquet
title: Stream output to a parquet file вҖ” sink_parquet
text: function allows stream LazyFrame larger RAM directly .parquet file without collecting R session, thus preventing crashes small memory.

title: Stream output to a parquet file вҖ” sink_parquet
code: sink_parquet(   .data,   path,   ...,   compression = "zstd",   compression_level = 3,   statistics = FALSE,   row_group_size = NULL,   data_page_size = NULL,   maintain_order = TRUE,   type_coercion = TRUE,   predicate_pushdown = TRUE,   projection_pushdown = TRUE,   simplify_expression = TRUE,   slice_pushdown = TRUE,   no_optimization = FALSE,   mkdir = FALSE )

title: Stream output to a parquet file вҖ” sink_parquet
text: .data Polars LazyFrame. path Output file. Can also partition_*() function export output multiple files (see Details section ). ... Ignored. compression compression method. One : "uncompressed" "zstd" (default): good compression performance "lz4": fast compression / decompression "snappy": backwards compatibility guarantees deal older parquet readers. "gzip", "lzo", "brotli" compression_level level compression use (default 3). used compression one "gzip", "brotli", "zstd". Higher compression means smaller files disk. "gzip" : min-level = 0, max-level = 10 "brotli" : min-level = 0, max-level = 11 "zstd" : min-level = 1, max-level = 22. statistics Whether compute write column statistics (default FALSE). requires computations. row_group_size Size row groups number rows. NULL (default), chunks DataFrame used. Writing smaller chunks may reduce memory pressure improve writing speeds. data_page_size NULL (default), limit around 1MB. maintain_order Whether maintain order data processed (default TRUE). Setting FALSE slightly faster. type_coercion Coerce types operations succeed run minimal required memory (default TRUE). predicate_pushdown Applies filters early possible scan level (default TRUE). projection_pushdown Select columns needed scan level (default TRUE). simplify_expression Various optimizations, constant folding replacing expensive operations faster alternatives (default TRUE). slice_pushdown load required slice scan. materialize sliced outputs level. materialize sliced outputs (default TRUE). no_optimization Sets following optimizations FALSE: predicate_pushdown, projection_pushdown,  slice_pushdown, simplify_expression. Default FALSE. mkdir Recursively create directories path.

title: Stream output to a parquet file вҖ” sink_parquet
text: input LazyFrame.

title: Stream output to a parquet file вҖ” sink_parquet
text: possible export data multiple files based various parameters, values variables, file maximum number rows. See partition_by() details.

title: Stream output to a parquet file вҖ” sink_parquet
code: # This is an example workflow where sink_parquet() is not very useful because # the data would fit in memory. It simply is an example of using it at the # end of a piped workflow.  # Create files for the CSV input and the Parquet output: file_csv <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") file_parquet <- tempfile(fileext = ".parquet")  # Write some data in a CSV file fake_data <- do.call("rbind", rep(list(mtcars), 1000)) write.csv(fake_data, file = file_csv, row.names = FALSE)  # In a new R session, we could read this file as a LazyFrame, do some operations, # and write it to a parquet file without ever collecting it in the R session: scan_csv_polars(file_csv) |>   filter(cyl %in% c(4, 6), mpg > 22) |>   mutate(     hp_gear_ratio = hp / gear   ) |>   sink_parquet(path = file_parquet)   #---------------------------------------------- # Write a LazyFrame to multiple files depending on various strategies. my_lf <- as_polars_lf(mtcars)  # Split the LazyFrame by key(s) and write each split to a different file: out_path <- withr::local_tempdir() sink_parquet(my_lf, partition_by_key(out_path, by = c("am", "cyl")), mkdir = TRUE) #> Warning: `partition_by_key()` was deprecated in tidypolars 0.16.0. #> в„№ Please use `partition_by(key = )` instead. fs::dir_tree(out_path) #> /tmp/RtmpcZqjs2/file190565022c32 #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ am=0.0 #> в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ cyl=4.0 #> в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.parquet #> в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ cyl=6.0 #> в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.parquet #> в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ cyl=8.0 #> в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.parquet #> в””в”Җв”Җ am=1.0 #>     в”ңв”Җв”Җ cyl=4.0 #>     в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.parquet #>     в”ңв”Җв”Җ cyl=6.0 #>     в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.parquet #>     в””в”Җв”Җ cyl=8.0 #>         в””в”Җв”Җ 00000000.parquet  # Split the LazyFrame by max number of rows per file: out_path <- withr::local_tempdir() sink_parquet(my_lf, partition_by_max_size(out_path, max_size = 5), mkdir = TRUE) #> Warning: `partition_by_max_size()` was deprecated in tidypolars 0.16.0. #> в„№ Please use `partition_by(max_rows_per_file = )` instead. fs::dir_tree(out_path) # mtcars has 32 rows so we have 7 output files #> /tmp/RtmpcZqjs2/file19057421bb9f #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ 00000000.parquet #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ 00000001.parquet #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ 00000002.parquet #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ 00000003.parquet #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ 00000004.parquet #> в”ңв”Җв”Җ 00000005.parquet #> в””в”Җв”Җ 00000006.parquet

text: .data Polars Data/LazyFrame ... Name-value pairs. name gives name column output. value can : vector length current group (whole data frame ungrouped). NULL, remove column. across() mostly supported, except cases. particular, .cols argument (...), select variables created across(). select helpers supported. See examples. .Optionally, selection columns group just operation, functioning alternative group_by(). group order maintained, use group_by() want control . .groups Grouping structure result. Must one : "drop_last" (default): drop last level grouping; "drop": levels grouping dropped; "keep": keep grouping structure .data. now, "rowwise" supported. Note dplyr uses .groups = NULL default, whose behavior depends number rows group output. However, returning several rows group summarize() deprecated (one use reframe() instead), .groups = NULL supported tidypolars.

title: tidypolars: More Efficient Tidyverse Code, Using Polars in the Background вҖ” tidypolars-package
text: Polars cross-language tool manipulating large data. However, one drawback R implementation syntax look odd many R users used Python syntax. objective tidypolars improve ease--use Polars R providing tidyverse syntax polars.

title: tidypolars: More Efficient Tidyverse Code, Using Polars in the Background вҖ” tidypolars-package
text: Maintainer: Etienne Bacher etienne.bacher@protonmail.com [copyright holder] contributors: Anatoly Tsyplenkov atsyplenkov@fastmail.com (ORCID) [contributor] Yousa Mirage Yousa-Mirage@foxmail.com (ORCID) (Yousa-Mirage) [contributor]

title: tidypolars global options вҖ” tidypolars_options
text: tidypolars following global options: tidypolars_unknown_args controls happens arguments passed expression unknown, e.g argument prob sample(). default ("warn") warns user arguments ignored tidypolars. accepted value "error" throw error happens. tidypolars_fallback_to_r controls happens unknown function (translated use polars syntax) passed expression. default FALSE, meaning unknown functions trigger error. Setting option TRUE convert data R, apply unknown function, convert output back polars. Using fallback R several drawbacks: loses polars built-parallelism optimizations; session may crash experience severe slowdown data converted R (especially input LazyFrame). package polars also contains several global options may useful, changing default behavior converting Int64 values R: https://pola-rs.github.io/r-polars/man/polars_options.html.

title: tidypolars global options вҖ” tidypolars_options
code: ##### Unknown arguments  options(tidypolars_unknown_args = "warn") test <- polars::pl$DataFrame(x = c(2, 1, 5, 3, 1))  # The default is to warn the user mutate(test, x2 = sample(x, prob = 0.5)) #> Warning: tidypolars doesn't know how to use some arguments of `sample()`. #> в„№ The following argument(s) will be ignored: "prob". #> shape: (5, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ x2  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 2.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 5.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # But one can make this stricter and throw an error when this happens options(tidypolars_unknown_args = "error") try(mutate(test, x2 = sample(x, prob = 0.5))) #> Error in mutate(test, x2 = sample(x, prob = 0.5)) :  #>   Error while running function `sample()` in Polars. #> вң– tidypolars doesn't know how to use some arguments of `sample()`: "prob". #> в„№ Use `options(tidypolars_unknown_args = "warn")` to warn when this happens #>   instead of throwing an error.  options(tidypolars_unknown_args = "warn")  ##### Fallback to R  test <- polars::pl$DataFrame(x = c(2, 1, 5, 3, 1))  # The default is to error because mad() isn't translated internally try(mutate(test, x2 = mad(x))) #> Error in mutate(test, x2 = mad(x)) :  #>   tidypolars doesn't know how to translate this function: `mad()` (from #> package stats). #> в„№ You can ask for it to be translated here: #>   <https://github.com/etiennebacher/tidypolars/issues>. #> в„№ See `?tidypolars_options` to set automatic fallback to R to handle unknown #>   functions.  # But one can allow fallback to R to apply this function and then convert # the output back to polars (see drawbacks in the "description" section # above) options(tidypolars_fallback_to_r = TRUE) mutate(test, x2 = mad(x)) #> shape: (5, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ x2     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ f64 в”Ҷ f64    в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 2.0 в”Ҷ 1.4826 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.4826 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 5.0 в”Ҷ 1.4826 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3.0 в”Ҷ 1.4826 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1.4826 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  options(tidypolars_fallback_to_r = FALSE)

code: test <- polars::pl$DataFrame(x = c("a", "b"), y = 100:101, n = c(1, 2)) test #> shape: (2, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ n   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ i32 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 100 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ b   в”Ҷ 101 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  uncount(test, n) #> shape: (3, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ i32 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 100 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ b   в”Ҷ 101 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ b   в”Ҷ 101 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  uncount(test, n, .id = "id") #> shape: (3, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ id  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ i32 в”Ҷ u32 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 100 в”Ҷ 1   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ b   в”Ҷ 101 в”Ҷ 1   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ b   в”Ҷ 101 в”Ҷ 2   в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # using constants uncount(test, 2) #> shape: (4, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ n   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ i32 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 100 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 100 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ b   в”Ҷ 101 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ b   в”Ҷ 101 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # using expressions uncount(test, 2 / n) #> shape: (3, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ x   в”Ҷ y   в”Ҷ n   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ i32 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 100 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ 100 в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ b   в”Ҷ 101 в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

text: data Polars Data/LazyFrame col name new column, string symbol. ... expression accepted dplyr::select(): variable names, column numbers, select helpers, etc. sep Separator use values. remove TRUE, remove input columns output Data/LazyFrame. na.rm TRUE, missing values replaced empty string prior uniting value.

title: Unite multiple columns into one by pasting strings together вҖ” unite.polars_data_frame
code: test <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   year = 2009:2011,   month = 10:12,   day = c(11L, 22L, 28L),   name_day = c("Monday", "Thursday", "Wednesday") )  # By default, united columns are dropped unite(test, col = "full_date", year, month, day, sep = "-") #> shape: (3, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ full_date  в”Ҷ name_day  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ ---        в”Ҷ ---       в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str        в”Ҷ str       в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 2009-10-11 в”Ҷ Monday    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2010-11-22 в”Ҷ Thursday  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2011-12-28 в”Ҷ Wednesday в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ unite(test, col = "full_date", year, month, day, sep = "-", remove = FALSE) #> shape: (3, 5) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ full_date  в”Ҷ year в”Ҷ month в”Ҷ day в”Ҷ name_day  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ ---        в”Ҷ ---  в”Ҷ ---   в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---       в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str        в”Ҷ i32  в”Ҷ i32   в”Ҷ i32 в”Ҷ str       в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 2009-10-11 в”Ҷ 2009 в”Ҷ 10    в”Ҷ 11  в”Ҷ Monday    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2010-11-22 в”Ҷ 2010 в”Ҷ 11    в”Ҷ 22  в”Ҷ Thursday  в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2011-12-28 в”Ҷ 2011 в”Ҷ 12    в”Ҷ 28  в”Ҷ Wednesday в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  test2 <- polars::pl$DataFrame(   name = c("John", "Jack", "Thomas"),   middlename = c("T.", NA, "F."),   surname = c("Smith", "Thompson", "Jones") )  # By default, NA values are kept in the character output unite(test2, col = "full_name", everything(), sep = " ") #> shape: (3, 1) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ full_name       в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ ---             в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str             в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ John T. Smith   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ Jack Thompson   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ Thomas F. Jones в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ unite(test2, col = "full_name", everything(), sep = " ", na.rm = TRUE) #> shape: (3, 1) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ full_name       в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ ---             в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ str             в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ John T. Smith   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ Jack Thompson   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ Thomas F. Jones в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

what: Unnest a list-column into rows вҖ” unnest_longer_polars
title: Unnest a list-column into rows вҖ” unnest_longer_polars
text: unnest_longer_polars() turns element list-column row. equivalent tidyr::unnest_longer().

title: Unnest a list-column into rows вҖ” unnest_longer_polars
code: unnest_longer_polars(   data,   col,   ...,   values_to = NULL,   indices_to = NULL,   keep_empty = FALSE )

title: Unnest a list-column into rows вҖ” unnest_longer_polars
text: data Polars DataFrame LazyFrame. col <tidy-select> Column(s) unnest. Can bare column names, character strings, tidyselect expressions. selecting multiple columns, list elements row must length across selected columns. ... dots future extensions must empty. values_to string giving column name store unnested values . NULL (default), original column name used. multiple columns selected, can glue string containing "{col}" provide template column names (e.g., values_to = "{col}_val"). indices_to string giving column name store index values. NULL (default), index column created. multiple columns selected, can glue string containing "{col}" create separate index columns unnested column (e.g., indices_to = "{col}_idx"). keep_empty TRUE, empty values (NULL empty lists) kept NA output. FALSE (default), empty values dropped.

title: Unnest a list-column into rows вҖ” unnest_longer_polars
text: Polars DataFrame LazyFrame list-column(s) unnested rows.

title: Unnest a list-column into rows вҖ” unnest_longer_polars
text: multiple columns selected, corresponding list elements row expanded together. requires selected columns lists length row. indices_to parameter creates integer column position (1-indexed) element within original list. Named elements list currently supported index names (use integer positions). using "{col}" templates multiple columns, template applied column name generate output column names.

title: Unnest a list-column into rows вҖ” unnest_longer_polars
code: library(polars)  # Basic example with a list column df <- pl$DataFrame(   id = 1:3,   values = list(c(1, 2), c(3, 4, 5), 6) ) df #> shape: (3, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ values          в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---             в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ list[f64]       в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ [1.0, 2.0]      в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ [3.0, 4.0, 5.0] в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3   в”Ҷ [6.0]           в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  unnest_longer_polars(df, values) #> shape: (6, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ values в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ f64    в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 1.0    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 2.0    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 3.0    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 4.0    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 5.0    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3   в”Ҷ 6.0    в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # With indices unnest_longer_polars(df, values, indices_to = "idx") #> shape: (6, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ idx в”Ҷ values в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ u32 в”Ҷ f64    в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 1   в”Ҷ 1.0    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 2   в”Ҷ 2.0    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 1   в”Ҷ 3.0    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 2   в”Ҷ 4.0    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 3   в”Ҷ 5.0    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3   в”Ҷ 1   в”Ҷ 6.0    в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # Rename the output column unnest_longer_polars(df, values, values_to = "val") #> shape: (6, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ val в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 4.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 5.0 в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3   в”Ҷ 6.0 в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # Multiple columns - list elements must have same length per row df2 <- pl$DataFrame(   id = 1:2,   a = list(c(1, 2), c(3, 4)),   b = list(c("x", "y"), c("z", "w")) ) unnest_longer_polars(df2, c(a, b)) #> shape: (4, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ a   в”Ҷ b   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ str в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ x   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ y   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ z   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 4.0 в”Ҷ w   в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # Multiple columns with values_to template unnest_longer_polars(df2, c(a, b), values_to = "{col}_val") #> shape: (4, 3) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ a_val в”Ҷ b_val в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---   в”Ҷ ---   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ f64   в”Ҷ str   в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 1.0   в”Ҷ x     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 2.0   в”Ҷ y     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 3.0   в”Ҷ z     в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 4.0   в”Ҷ w     в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # Multiple columns with indices_to template unnest_longer_polars(df2, c(a, b), indices_to = "{col}_idx") #> shape: (4, 5) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ a_idx в”Ҷ a   в”Ҷ b_idx в”Ҷ b   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---   в”Ҷ --- в”Ҷ ---   в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ u32   в”Ҷ f64 в”Ҷ u32   в”Ҷ str в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 1     в”Ҷ 1.0 в”Ҷ 1     в”Ҷ x   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 2     в”Ҷ 2.0 в”Ҷ 2     в”Ҷ y   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 1     в”Ҷ 3.0 в”Ҷ 1     в”Ҷ z   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ 2     в”Ҷ 4.0 в”Ҷ 2     в”Ҷ w   в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # keep_empty example df4 <- pl$DataFrame(   id = 1:3,   values = list(c(1, 2), NULL, 3) )  # By default, NULL/empty values are dropped unnest_longer_polars(df4, values) #> shape: (3, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ values в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ f64    в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 1.0    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 2.0    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3   в”Ҷ 3.0    в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  # Use keep_empty = TRUE to keep them as NA unnest_longer_polars(df4, values, keep_empty = TRUE) #> shape: (4, 2) #> в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ #> в”Ӯ id  в”Ҷ values в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ i32 в”Ҷ f64    в”Ӯ #> в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 1.0    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ 2.0    в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ null   в”Ӯ #> в”Ӯ 3   в”Ҷ 3.0    в”Ӯ #> в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

what: Export data to CSV file(s) вҖ” write_csv_polars
title: Export data to CSV file(s) вҖ” write_csv_polars
text: Export data CSV file(s)

title: Export data to CSV file(s) вҖ” write_csv_polars
code: write_csv_polars(   .data,   file,   ...,   include_bom = FALSE,   include_header = TRUE,   separator = ",",   line_terminator = "\n",   quote_char = "\"",   batch_size = 1024,   datetime_format = NULL,   date_format = NULL,   time_format = NULL,   float_precision = NULL,   null_value = "",   quote_style = "necessary",   quote,   null_values )

title: Export data to CSV file(s) вҖ” write_csv_polars
text: .data Polars DataFrame. file File path result written. ... Ignored. include_bom Whether include UTF-8 BOM (byte order mark) CSV output. include_header Whether include header CSV output. separator Separate CSV fields symbol. line_terminator String used end row. quote_char Byte use quoting character. batch_size Number rows processed per thread. datetime_format format string, specifiers defined chrono Rust crate. format specified, default fractional-second precision inferred maximum timeunit found frameвҖҷs Datetime cols (). date_format format string, specifiers defined chrono Rust crate. time_format format string, specifiers defined chrono Rust crate. float_precision Number decimal places write, applied Float32 Float64 datatypes. null_value string representing null values (defaulting empty string). quote_style Determines quoting strategy used. "necessary" (default): puts quotes around fields necessary. necessary fields contain quote, delimiter record terminator. Quotes also necessary writing empty record (indistinguishable record one empty field). default. "always": puts quotes around every field. "non_numeric": puts quotes around fields non-numeric. Namely, writing field parse valid float integer, quotes used even aren`t strictly necessary. "never": never puts quotes around fields, even results invalid CSV data (e.g. quoting strings containing separator). quote Deprecated, use quote_char instead. null_values Deprecated, use null_value instead.

title: Export data to CSV file(s) вҖ” write_csv_polars
text: input DataFrame.

title: Export data to CSV file(s) вҖ” write_csv_polars
text: possible export data multiple files based various parameters, values variables, file maximum number rows. See partition_by() details.

title: Export data to IPC file(s) вҖ” write_ipc_polars
text: .data Polars DataFrame. file File path result written. compression NULL character compression method, "uncompressed" "lz4" "zstd". NULL equivalent "uncompressed". Choose "zstd" good compression performance. Choose "lz4" fast compression/decompression. ... Ignored. compat_level Determines compatibility level exporting Polars' internal data structures. specifying new compatibility level, Polars exports internal data structures might interpretable Arrow implementations. level can specified name (e.g., "newest") scalar integer (currently, 0 1 supported). "newest" (default): Use highest level, currently 1 (Low compatibility). "oldest": 0 (High compatibility). future Deprecated, use compat_level instead.

title: Export data to Parquet file(s) вҖ” write_parquet_polars
text: .data Polars DataFrame. file File path result written. ... Ignored. compression compression method. One : "uncompressed" "zstd" (default): good compression performance "lz4": fast compression / decompression "snappy": backwards compatibility guarantees deal older parquet readers. "gzip", "lzo", "brotli" compression_level level compression use (default 3). used compression one "gzip", "brotli", "zstd". Higher compression means smaller files disk. "gzip" : min-level = 0, max-level = 10 "brotli" : min-level = 0, max-level = 11 "zstd" : min-level = 1, max-level = 22. statistics Whether compute write column statistics (default FALSE). requires computations. row_group_size Size row groups number rows. NULL (default), chunks DataFrame used. Writing smaller chunks may reduce memory pressure improve writing speeds. data_page_size NULL (default), limit around 1MB. partition_by Column(s) partition . partitioned dataset written specified. partition_chunk_size_bytes Approximate size split DataFrames within single partition writing. Note calculated using size DataFrame memory - size output file may differ depending file format / compression. mkdir Recursively create directories path.

tidypolars 0.17.0
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 1.9.0 dplyr >= 1.2.0.

title: tidypolars 0.17.0
text: following functions (deprecated since 0.10.0, August 2024) now removed (#303): describe(), use summary() instead. describe_plan() describe_optimized_plan(), use explain(optimized = TRUE/FALSE) instead. make_unique_id() deprecated removed future version. underlying Polars function isnвҖҷt guaranteed give results across different versions. function doesnвҖҷt replacement tidypolars (#304). partition_by_key() partition_by_max_size() (already deprecated 0.16.0), argument per_partition_sort_by removed (#322).

title: tidypolars 0.17.0
text: Added support dplyr::near() (#311). pivot_wider() now works Polars LazyFrames (#318). Added support several functions implemented dplyr 1.2.0: filter_out() (#280) recode_values() (#308) replace_values() (#308) replace_when() (#307) when_any() (#306) when_all() (#306) separate() now supports regex sep argument (#320).

title: tidypolars 0.17.0
text: Several changes make tidypolars aligned tidyverse output general (#316): count(), sort = TRUE ties, variables sorted increasing order. coalesce() longer default argument. implementation mistake since dplyr::coalesce() never argument. ungroup() used remove group-specific attributes original grouped data, even result operation assigned. fixed. replace_na() Polars DataFrame LazyFrame now errors replacement list. slice_*() functions grouped data return columns order input. summarize() NULL expressions now returns one row per unique group instead entire data. unite() now returns columns correct order, doesnвҖҷt duplicate sep output values NA.

title: tidypolars 0.17.0
text: bind_rows_polars() now uses input names .id inputs named, example bind_rows_polars(x1 = x1, x2, .id = "id") (#317).

title: tidypolars 0.16.0
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 1.8.0.

title: tidypolars 0.16.0
text: New function unnest_longer_polars() unnest list-columns rows, equivalent tidyr::unnest_longer(). supports parameters values_to, indices_to, keep_empty, well {col} templates column naming. (#212, #281, @Yousa-Mirage) New functions separate_longer_delim_polars() separate_longer_position_polars() split string columns rows delimiter fixed width, equivalent tidyr::separate_longer_delim() tidyr::separate_longer_position(). (#57, #285, @Yousa-Mirage) New argument .fill() (introduced tidyr 1.3.2). (#283) wday() now supports arbitrary week_start values (1~7), allowing custom week start days. (#292, @Yousa-Mirage) Add support argument type nchar (#288). now possible use translated functions without loading package come . example, following code can run without loading stringr session: can useful benefit polars speed using interface tidyverse functions, without adding additional tidyverse dependencies. may useful avoid installing extra dependencies, recommended usage makes harder convert tidypolars code run tidyverse-based backends. information ?.tp (#293). New argument mkdir write_parquet_polars() (already existed sink_parquet()). (#298) New (experimental) function partition_by() write partitioned output sink_*() write_*_polars(). following functions deprecated removed future release (#299): partition_by_key() can replaced partition_by(key =) partition_by_max_size() can replaced partition_by(max_rows_per_file =)
code: data |>   mutate(y = .tp$str_extract_stringr(x, "\\d+"))

title: tidypolars 0.16.0
text: collect() now returns tibble instead data.frame, consistency collect() methods (#273).

title: tidypolars 0.16.0
text: arrange() now works literal values, arrange(x, 1:2) (#296).

title: tidypolars 0.16.0
text: Removed вҖңFAQвҖқ vignette, outdated wasnвҖҷt particularly helpful.

title: tidypolars 0.15.1
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 1.6.0.

title: tidypolars 0.15.0
text: consistency dplyr, distinct() now keeps selected columns. keep columns, use .keep_all = TRUE (#227, @ppanko).

title: tidypolars 0.15.0
text: New argument mkdir sink_*() functions recursively create folder(s) specified path(s) files (#236). New functions partition_by_key() partition_by_max_size() can used path argument sink_*() functions. enable writing LazyFrame several files partitioned output. See details ?sink_parquet() (#237). bind_cols_polars() now works two LazyFrames (#244). Add support gsub() (#250). Add partial support stringr::str_equal() (#228). Add support lubridate functions rollbackward(), rollback(), rollforward() (#252). Support stringr::fixed() stringr functions (#250). Add support argument ignore.case grepl() (#251). Add support argument .keep_all distinct() (#227, @ppanko).

title: tidypolars 0.15.0
text: Better error message group_by() unsupported argument .drop (#230). Better error message group_by() passing named expressions .... dplyr supports recommended use ./ argument individual functions rather using group_by() ungroup() (#238). Better error message count() passing named expressions ... (#239). Fix bug join_where() common column names two DataFrames used join conditions (#254). Using %% NA now retains NA data. Using %% NA error (#256). Remove occasional deprecation message coming Polars using %% (#259, @ppanko). Better handling functions prefixed <pkg>:: (#261). Fix wrong behavior paste() paste0() collapse (#263).

title: tidypolars 0.15.0
text: New vignette вҖңbenchmark tidypolarsвҖқ (#232). Better documentation read_*() scan_*() functions (#241).

title: tidypolars 0.14.1
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 1.1.0 (#222).

title: tidypolars 0.14.1
text: Fix corner case filter() used custom function missing arguments (#220). grepl(), argument fixed now used correctly (thanks @gernophil report, #223). if_else() ifelse() now work using named arguments (#224).

title: tidypolars 0.14.0
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 1.0.0. release polars contains many breaking changes. invisible tidypolars users, exception deprecation messages (see ). However, code contains user-defined functions use polars syntax, may need revise (#194).

title: tidypolars 0.14.0
text: following arguments deprecated removed future version. recommended replacement indicated right arrow (#194): compute() collect(): streaming -> engine; dtypes -> schema_overrides reuse_downloaded -> replacement reuse_downloaded -> replacement memory_map -> replacement null_values -> null_value quote -> quote_char pretty -> replacement row_oriented -> replacement future -> compat_level fetch() deprecated, use head() collect() instead (#194). group_keys() now returns tibble data.frame anymore (#194). lubridate::make_date(), lubridate::make_datetime(), ISOdatetime() now error components go expected range, e.g.В month = 20 hour = 25. , functions returning NA situation (#194). summary() returns additional row 50% percentile (#194).

title: tidypolars 0.14.0
text: Added support various lubridate functions: force_tz() with_tz() (@atsyplenkov, #170); date() (@atsyplenkov, #181); today() now() (#183); weeks(), days(), hours(), minutes(), seconds(), milliseconds(), microseconds(), nanoseconds() (#184). tidypolars can now use expressions contain non-translated functions expressions use columns data. Example: agrep() translated function used error: However, see agrep("", ) doesnвҖҷt use column instead object environment can evaluated without caring whether tidypolars knows function : Note evaluated running polars background expression canвҖҷt benefit polars parallel evaluation instance. Thanks @mgacc0 suggestion. Add support .Date() character columns (#190). Error messages due untranslated functions now suggest opening issue ask translation (#197). Add support %>% expressions (#200). Add support dplyr::tally() (#203). count() add_count() now warn error argument wt used since supported. behavior depends global option tidypolars_unknown_args (#204). tidypolars experimental support fallback R function internally translated polars syntax. default behavior still error, user can now set options(tidypolars_fallback_to_r = TRUE) handle unknown functions. See ?tidypolars_options details drawbacks approach (#205). Large performance improvement using selection helpers (contains()) data many columns (#211). tidypolars now exports rules used flir detecting deprecated functions describe_plan() describe_optimized_plan(). can used project following article. Note requires flir 0.5.0.9000 higher (#214).
code: dat <- pl$DataFrame(foo = c(2, 1, 2)) a <- c("d", "e", "f") dat |>   filter(foo >= agrep("a", a)) Error in `filter()`: ! `tidypolars` doesn't know how to translate this function: `agrep()`. shape: (1, 1) в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ foo в”Ӯ в”Ӯ --- в”Ӯ в”Ӯ f64 в”Ӯ в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў в”Ӯ 2.0 в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

title: tidypolars 0.14.0
text: Fix behavior mutate() summarize() donвҖҷt contain expression (#191). Fix error count() includes grouping variables (#193). Passing . anonymous function across() now works (#216).

title: tidypolars 0.13.0
text: Added support stringr::str_replace_na() (#153). Better checks unknown unsupported arguments compute(), collect(), *_join(), pivot_*(), sink_*(), slice_sample() uncount()(#158, thanks @fkohrt report). Now, functions receive: argument exists tidyverse implementation supported tidypolars, warn user. default behaviour can changed error instead options(tidypolars_unknown_args = "error"). argument doesnвҖҷt exist , error. Add support argument explicit tidyr::complete(). Add option keep track filenames scan_csv_polars() (#171, @ginolhac). Add partial support seq() (argument length.supported) seq_len(). complete() now accepts named elements, e.g.В complete(df, group, value = 1:4) (#176). Add support several lubridate functions: (), pm(), leap_year(), days_in_month() (#178);

title: tidypolars 0.13.0
text: Fix edge cases tidypolars implementation stringr::str_sub() substr() compared original implementation (#159). arrange() now places NA values last, like dplyr.

title: tidypolars 0.12.0
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 0.21.0.

title: tidypolars 0.12.0
text: summarize() now drops last group output default (consistency dplyr). Previously kept groups input data (#149).

title: tidypolars 0.12.0
text: Add support argument .groups summarize(). Value "rowwise" supported now (#149). Added support dplyr::lead(). dplyr::lead() dplyr::lag(), arguments default order_by now supported (#151).

title: tidypolars 0.11.0
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 0.20.0.

title: tidypolars 0.11.0
text: arrange() now errors unknown variable names (like dplyr::arrange()). Previously, unknown variables silently ignored. Using expressions (like + b) now accepted (#144). parameter inherit_optimization removed sink_*() functions.

title: tidypolars 0.11.0
text: power operators ^ ** now work. New function sink_ndjson() write results lazy query NDJSON file without collecting memory. inner_join() now accepts inequality joins argument, including following helpers: (), overlaps(), within() (#148).

title: tidypolars 0.11.0
text: Using external object case_when(), ifelse() ifelse() now works. str_sub() doesnвҖҷt error anymore start positive end negative. read_*_polars() functions used return standard data.frame mistake. now return Polars DataFrame. Using [ subsetting expressions now works. Thanks @ginolhac report (#141). bind_cols_polars() bind_rows_polars() now error (expected ) elements mix Polars DataFrames LazyFrames.

title: tidypolars 0.10.1
text: error handling columns datatype Null. Note converting columns R .data.frame(), as_tibble(), collect() still issue polars 0.19.1.

title: tidypolars 0.10.0
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 0.19.1.

title: tidypolars 0.10.0
text: describe() deprecated tidypolars 0.10.0 removed future update. Use summary() arguments instead (#127). describe_plan() describe_optimized_plan() deprecated tidypolars 0.10.0 removed future update. Use explain() optimized = TRUE/FALSE instead (#128). sink_parquet() sink_csv(), arguments except .data path must named (#136).

title: tidypolars 0.10.0
text: Add support functions: package base: substr(). Better error message function can come several packages one version translated (#130). row_number() now works without argument (#131). New functions import data Polars DataFrames LazyFrames (#136): read_<format>_polars() import data Polars DataFrame; scan_<format>_polars() import data Polars LazyFrame; <format> can вҖңcsvвҖқ, вҖңipcвҖқ, вҖңjsonвҖқ, вҖңparquetвҖқ. can replace functions polars. example, polars::pl$read_parquet(...) can replaced read_parquet_polars(...). New functions write Polars DataFrames external files: write_<format>_polars() <format> can вҖңcsvвҖқ, вҖңipcвҖқ, вҖңjsonвҖқ, вҖңndjsonвҖқ, вҖңparquetвҖқ (#136). New function sink_ipc() similar sink_parquet() sink_csv() IPC files (#136). across() now throws better error message user passes external list .fns. works dplyr work tidypolars (#135). Added support argument .add group_by().

title: tidypolars 0.10.0
text: stringr::str_sub() now works start end negative. Fixed bug str_sub() start greater 1. stringr::str_starts() stringr::str_ends() now work regex. fill() doesnвҖҷt error anymore ... empty. Instead, returns input data. unite() now provides proper error message col missing. unite() doesnвҖҷt error anymore ... empty. Instead, uses variables dataset. filter(), mutate() summarize() now work using column another data.frame, e.g. replace_na() longer converts column datatype replacement, e.g.В data |> replace_na("") error input data numeric. n_distinct() now correctly applies na.rm argument several columns passed input (#137).
code: my_polars_df |>   filter(x %in% some_data_frame$y)

title: tidypolars 0.9.0
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 0.18.0.

title: tidypolars 0.9.0
text: Add support several functions: package base: %% %/%. package dplyr: dense_rank(), row_number(). package lubridate: wday(). Better handling missing values match R behavior. following functions, least one missing value na.rm = FALSE (default), output NA: max(), mean(), median(), min(), sd(), sum(), var() (#120). New argument cluster_with_columns collect(), compute(), fetch(). Add global option tidypolars_unknown_args control happens tidypolars doesnвҖҷt know handle argument function. default warn accepted value "error".

title: tidypolars 0.9.0
text: count() add_count() longer overwrite variable named n argument name unspecified.

title: tidypolars 0.8.0
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 0.17.0.

title: tidypolars 0.8.0
text: announced tidypolars 0.7.0, behavior collect() changed. now returns standard R data.frame Polars DataFrame anymore. Replace collect() compute() (arguments) keep old behavior. bind_rows_polars(), .id passed, resulting column now type character instead integer.

title: tidypolars 0.8.0
text: Add support several functions: package base: (), (), diff(), ISOdatetime(), length(), rev(), unique(). package dplyr: consecutive_id(), min_rank(), na_if(), n_distinct(), nth(). package lubridate: make_datetime(). package stringr: str_dup(), str_split(), str_split_i(), str_trunc(). package tidyr: replace_na() (data.frame method already translated vector one can used mutate() example). now possible use explicit namespaces (dplyr::first() instead first()) mutate(), summarize() filter() (#114). bind_rows_polars(), elements named .id specified, .id column use names elements (#116). now possible rename variables select() (#117). Add support argument na_matches join functions (except cross_join() doesnвҖҷt need ) (#109).

title: tidypolars 0.8.0
text: Local variables custom functions used tidypolars functions (reported blog post Art Steinmetz). now fixed. across() now works .cols contains one variable .fns contains one function. across(), .cols argument now takes account variables created mutate() summarize() call across(). Note () function supported . example: return 0 variable foo. warning emitted behavior. Better handling negative values c() called mutate() summarize().

title: tidypolars 0.7.0
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 0.16.0.

title: tidypolars 0.7.0
text: as_polars() now removed. deprecated 0.6.0. Use as_polars_df() as_polars_lf() instead. to_r() now removed. deprecated 0.6.0. Use .data.frame() as_tibble() instead. consistency dplyr, behavior collect() change 0.8.0 perform lazy query convert result standard data.frame. now, collect() throws warning future change. recommended use compute() perform query get Polars DataFrame output (#101).

title: tidypolars 0.7.0
text: Several improvements changes pivot_wider() (#95): names_from can now takes several variables; add support id_cols names_glue; default value names_sep now _, consistency tidyr; fix documentation pivot_wider() doesnвҖҷt work LazyFrame. Add support stringr::regex(). Note argument ignore_case supported now (#97). Add support several lubridate functions: dweeks(), ddays(), dhours(), dminutes(), dseconds(), dmilliseconds(), make_date() (#107). polars function called internally fails, original error message now displayed. Add support group_split() (DataFrame ). Add support argument relationship left_join(), right_join(), full_join() inner_join() (#106).

title: tidypolars 0.6.0
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 0.15.0.

title: tidypolars 0.6.0
text: as_polars() deprecated removed 0.7.0. Use as_polars_lf() as_polars_df() instead. as_polars() doesnвҖҷt argument with_string_cache anymore. set TRUE, enabled string cache globally, lead undesirable side effects. to_r() deprecated removed 0.7.0. Use .data.frame() as_tibble() instead. used silently return LazyFrame input LazyFrame. now automatically collects LazyFrame (#88). pull() nows automatically collects input LazyFrame (#89).

title: tidypolars 0.6.0
text: Add support argument .keep mutate() (#80). Add support group_vars() group_keys() (#81). Experimental support rowwise(). now, limited functions: mean(), median(), min(), max(), sum(), (), (). rowwise() group_by() used time (#40). functions return polars Data/LazyFrame now add class "tidypolars" output (#86). Support .min(), .max(), dplyr::n(). Support .data[[ .env[[ addition .data$ .env$. Better error messages objects specified .data .env donвҖҷt exist.

title: tidypolars 0.6.0
text: pull() now errors var length > 1.

title: tidypolars 0.5.0

title: tidypolars 0.5.0
text: across() now errors argument .cols provided (either named unnamed). behavior deprecated dplyr 1.1.0. longer possible use ! arrange() sort decreasing order, compatibility dplyr::arrange(). Use - desc() instead.

title: tidypolars 0.5.0
text: summarize() now works ungrouped data returns 1-row output. now possible use desc(x1) arrange() sort decreasing order x1 (equivalent -x1). Add support argument names_prefix pivot_longer(). Add support arguments names_prefix names_sep pivot_wider(). Add support tidyr::uncount(). *_join() functions now work specification created dplyr::join_by(). Notice limited equality joins now. can now use вҖңembraceвҖқ operator {{ }} pass unquoted column names (among things) arguments custom functions. See вҖңProgramming dplyrвҖқ vignette examples. bind_cols_polars() now works two LazyFrames, . Add support argument .name_repair bind_cols_polars() (#74). Support .env$ .data$ pronouns expressions filter(), mutate() summarize(). Support named vector argument pattern str_replace_all(), names patterns values replacements. Using %% factor variables doesnвҖҷt require enabling string cache anymore.

title: tidypolars 0.5.0
text: summarize() longer errors across(everything(), ...) used .. *_join() functions longer error named vector provided argument . Expressions values named вҖңliteralвҖқ anymore.

title: tidypolars 0.5.0
text: Simplify procedure support new functions.

title: tidypolars 0.4.0
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 0.11.0.

title: tidypolars 0.4.0
text: longer possible pass list rename().

title: tidypolars 0.4.0
text: argument with_string_cache as_polars() now enables string cache globally set TRUE (#54). Better error message filter() comparing factors strings string cache disabled. Basic support strptime(). possible use strptime(*, strict = FALSE) error parsing characters fails. New argument .filter(), mutate(), summarize(), new argument slice_*() functions. allows operations groups without using group_by() ungroup(). See dplyr vignette information (#59). rename() now accepts unquoted names old new names. Support fixed regexes str_detect() (using fixed()) grepl() (using fixed = TRUE).

title: tidypolars 0.4.0
text: Improve robustness sequential expressions mutate() summarize() (.e expressions run one depend variables created call) (#58). relocate() now works correctly .= last_col(). functions work grouped data now correctly restore groups structure (#62).

title: tidypolars 0.4.0
text: Error messages coming mutate(), summarize(), filter() now give right function call. Faster tidy selection (#61).

title: tidypolars 0.3.0
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 0.10.0.

title: tidypolars 0.3.0
text: functions starting pl_ removed benefit S3 methods. example, pl_distinct() doesnвҖҷt exist anymore way use load dplyr use distinct() Polars DataFrame LazyFrame. avoid confusion compatibility dplyr tidyr. See #49 detailed explanation. pl_bind_rows() pl_bind_cols() renamed bind_rows_polars() bind_cols_polars() respectively. bind_rows() bind_cols() S3 methods (might change future versions dplyr).

title: tidypolars 0.3.0
text: New function duplicated_rows() opposite distinct() (#50). New argument .id bind_rows_polars(). bind_rows_polars() can now bind Data/LazyFrames donвҖҷt schema. Columns upcast common types necessary. Unknown columns filled NA.

title: tidypolars 0.3.0
text: complete() now works correctly grouped data.

title: tidypolars 0.3.0
text: relig_income fish_encounters reexported anymore since tidyr now imported.

title: tidypolars 0.2.0
text: tidypolars requires polars >= 0.9.0.

title: tidypolars 0.2.0
text: Rename pl_fetch() fetch(). New functions supported: describe(), sink_csv(), slice_sample(). New argument fill pl_complete(). Support stringr::str_to_title() tools::toTitleCase(). Support stringr::fixed() use literal strings. Support replacements captured groups like \\1 stringr::str_replace() stringr::str_replace_all().

title: tidypolars 0.2.0
text: sink_parquet() didnвҖҷt use user inputs (apart path).

title: tidypolars 0.2.0
text: Clearer error message expression contains <pkg>::. supported now potentially implemented later. pl_colnames() longer exported.

title: tidypolars 0.1.0
text: Support .numeric(), .character(), .logical(), grepl(), paste() expressions pl_filter(), pl_mutate() pl_summarize(). Support sink_parquet() (#38). Support fetch() (#42). Support additional stringr functions: str_detect(), str_extract_all(), str_pad(), str_squish(), str_trim(), word() (arguments corner cases supported yet). Add optimization parameters collect().

title: tidypolars 0.1.0
text: Fix pl_mutate() pl_summarize() expressions use variables previously created modified (#10, #37). Fix bug pl_filter() passing vector RHS %%.

title: tidypolars 0.1.0
text: Improve backend translate R expressions Polars expressions. also led complete rewriting vignette вҖңR Polars expressionsвҖқ (#27). Error messages now report correct function call. Improve CI coverage (#35).

title: tidypolars 0.0.1
text: First Github release.
